I really like the game: I enjoy the combat styles of most characters and the visuals are appealing. My biggest issue is that the game is very unfriendly to free-to-play players. For example, it is nearly impossible for F2P players to obtain the Radiant Potion of Charm — except through the Time Space Junction or if the developers are generous; otherwise it is only available via draws or the bundle shop, which in both cases means spending money. The pets are similarly difficult to acquire, either due to low drop chances or because they simply despawn. In addition, once you have completed all content as an F2P player, you can only obtain Star Fragments through daily tasks — at only 120 per day, you need three days to afford a single draw. Overall it is a very good game, but I will most likely stop playing it soon, as many others already have.

I’ve spent a ton of hours on this game over the past ~7 weeks, so I figured I’d share my thoughts.  I originally started playing because I’m a huge *Seven Deadly Sins* fan—I love the characters and the world. What surprised me most is that the game doesn’t feel like a lazy cash grab. It actually feels like something made for the community. The story doesn’t follow a predictable path, and I genuinely can’t tell what’s going to happen next, which keeps it interesting.  As a hardcore fan, I won’t say I love the story as much as the anime, but it’s still good. It hints at some really interesting directions, and for me, that’s enough—it’s not amazing, but definitely not bad.  Gameplay is where the game really shines. It’s not just mindless button smashing—you actually have to think about your abilities, team composition, and how you gear your characters depending on the enemy.  The gacha system is surprisingly fair. You can focus on characters you like, and the game gives enough in-game currency that you can realistically max them out over time. Personally, I’m saving for Elizabeth.  Exploration is also fun—you can go around completing maps, collecting chests, and pushing toward 100% completion. That said, achievements are… rough. Some of them require insane amounts of time (like thousands of minutes of farming/AFKing), and it can feel more exhausting than rewarding.  There’s also a *lot* of grinding. Early on, you can breeze through content if you’re good, but eventually you’ll hit a wall and need to farm for hours to progress. Then once you catch up, you basically become overpowered until the next update.  Even with all that, the game has given me a lot of fun moments and great memories—and it’s still just getting started. I’m really curious to see what Netmarble does with it next.  If you’ve been thinking about trying it, I’d say give it a few hours. You’ll quickly figure out whether it’s for you—but it’s definitely not a waste of time.

Hello everyone who is reading this.  I'm writing this review after spending 465.6 hours in the game, and believe me — I have a lot to say. I'm changing my review from negative to positive because I can see that the game has improved. However, there are still several issues that continue to frustrate both me and my friends.  I want to explain what bothered me before and how Patch 1.5 addressed some of those problems. I genuinely hope the developers read player feedback and continue working on improvements.  First, I'll talk about the issues that were fixed, and at the end I'll list the TOP 4 most frustrating problems that still exist in the game.  1. Bosses running all over the arena (Galan, Spider Queen, Ferzen...)  I previously wrote that I came to fight a boss, not chase it around the entire map. Thankfully, I can now see some improvements. The Spider Queen and Ferzen's Mine are currently unavailable, and I hope that when they return, the bosses won't be running from one side of the arena to the other.  As for Galan, I noticed that his dash attack (sometimes one, two, or even three dashes in a row), where he leaves behind a clone that pulls the player, now has varying distances. Previously, these dashes covered an extremely long distance and were incredibly annoying. Now he can sometimes dash only about a meter, which is great because players don't have to spend half the fight running after him.  It's a small improvement, but I really appreciate it.  The new raid boss, Taranis, is also quite good because he doesn't constantly jump around the arena. He stays close to the player and actually allows you to hit him. In my opinion, this is the most balanced boss in terms of animations and mechanics.  (Except for his HP pool. I think it's a bit excessive, but since it's a raid boss, I'll let it slide.)  2. Sets and Engravings  Allowing players to use armor sets and engravings simultaneously was an excellent decision.  This increased build diversity and also improved survivability through additional HP and defense.  As a result, bosses in Timespace Junction no longer feel overwhelmingly punishing, where they could kill you in just 2–3 hits. I think this is a very positive change.  3. Farming Elite Bosses Without Keys  This is another great decision.  Players can now level multiple characters more comfortably. Previously, this was a major issue because you wanted to level characters while also collecting boss rewards, but there were never enough keys for everything.  Now the process feels much better. I'm satisfied with this change.  4. Double Rewards  Adding an extra reward to boss fights was a good decision, and having a chance to receive double rewards saves a lot of time.  Farming specific sets feels much less tedious now, which is definitely a positive improvement.  5. Crafting System and Workbench Menu  The workbench interface is significantly more convenient than it used to be.  The crafting system is mostly in a good state. Adding new craftable armor sets and accessories through the workbench was a smart decision, and I'm happy to see it.  6. Rewards  I'm pleased that the developers sent standard lottery summons through the in-game mail, since they're extremely difficult to obtain elsewhere.  We'll see how things develop moving forward.  TOP 4 Most Frustrating Problems That Still Exist 1. You Can't See Anything  The giant damage numbers and visual effects often completely cover the boss.  When dealing damage-over-time effects or high attack frequency characters (such as Merlin with a shared ultimate, Daisy with a Grimoire, Escanor with a sword, or Clotho with a staff), the screen becomes flooded with damage numbers and visual effects.  At that point, you can barely see the boss — sometimes you can't even see half the screen.  You end up attacking and dodging based purely on instinct rather than actually reacting to what the boss is doing.  This becomes a serious problem during difficult encounters where players need to watch boss mechanics and react appropriately, instead of staring at a wall of numbers.  Possible solutions:  Allow players to completely disable damage numbers. Add transparency settings for damage numbers and ultimate animations. Reduce the size of damage numbers and move them higher on the screen so they don't interfere with combat.  I talk with many players, and everyone says the same thing:  "We can't see anything."  2. The Game Provides Very Little Useful Information  The Timespace Junction description basically says:  "Here are the two elements this boss is weak against."  And that's it. Good luck spending the next 30 runs figuring out how the boss actually works.  There are no detailed boss descriptions. No explanations of mechanics. No information about strengths or weaknesses.  For example, the Dragon in Sector 4 has 300% Defense, and this defense can be removed through frequent Ultimate attacks. However, this information is written nowhere in the game.  Why is this a problem?  Because when I enter with fully geared, top-tier characters and see almost no damage being dealt, I feel like a complete beginner who accidentally walked into endgame content with underleveled characters.  That experience is neither enjoyable nor rewarding.  Players should not be forced to use third-party websites just to understand basic game mechanics. The game itself should provide that information.  I'd also like to see information about engravings, armor sets, and accessories included in the Codex instead of having to run to the engraving vendor every time I want to check something.  3. Server and Ping Issues  Both my friends and I constantly experience connection problems.  The connection indicator is almost always red. Occasionally it turns yellow. I've never once seen it green, despite having a very fast internet connection.  I don't know what's happening with the game's servers, but the high ping significantly impacts gameplay, especially when precise dodging is required.  Those who have experienced it know exactly what I'm talking about.  4. Training Dummy and Statistics  The game lacks a proper training dummy where players can test their builds and characters.  The single practice enemy currently available doesn't provide any useful statistics, making it impossible to properly test damage output under conditions similar to bosses, Timespace Junction encounters, or raids.  It's often difficult to determine which armor set, engraving, or stat configuration is actually better.  The game needs a detailed testing environment with:  Adjustable training dummy settings DPS tracking Maximum hit damage tracking Attack frequency statistics Detailed combat analysis  Without proper testing tools, players may unknowingly build their characters incorrectly.  And once again, because of the lack of information, players are forced to rely on external websites or YouTube guides, which is frustrating.  Final Thoughts  Overall, I am satisfied with Update 1.5.  The previous update was so bad that I was ready to uninstall the game.  The most important thing is that developers find a balance and understand that player comfort is more important than making dungeons and bosses excessively difficult.  Because when players cannot complete content or overcome challenges due to frustration rather than skill, the game stops being fun.  And if a game isn't fun, what's the point of playing it?  Thank you.

An anime Gacha game with a very satisfying gameplay loop in the endgame (in my opinion). This is the only gacha game I'm actually playing, so I can't really compare it to others.  Controls are smooth, combat is satisfying and easy enough to quickly get into, but with lots of depth for item and team comp building once you dig into it, without bothering you with it until late in the game, so when starting out you can sort of just play without too much mental burden and slowly get a feeling for it, which is very nice.  I'm not much of a graphic person, but the visuals are nice. The game world is huge, beautiful and very detailed. The characters look cool, faithful to the anime and animations and voice acting are on point.  Now the obvious and important stuff: When starting out you get lots of resources very quickly, like in any gacha and everything is very generous. Ofc, down the line after you completed the (quite long, but fun and entertaining) main story, you will receive less stuff and are mostly bound to a daily income, which is actually very nice to me, as this doesn't force you to play hours upon hours each day. You can actually get done with the truely relevant and important daily stuff in like 30mins or even faster and you won't feel bad like you are missing out on something. Its very nice if you do not have as much time for gaming. However, some players who want to invest more time into the game dislike this, which I understand. I guess it depends on yourself.  Update: You can now even bundle your daily resource to spend in a box and suspend it for other days if you don't feel like playing today. So you just log in, grab the login reward and bundle everything up and keep it for another day when you feel like playing more.  Since launch the Devs fixed pretty much all of the bigger issues of the game with their 1.1 and 1.2 patches and now I can easily recommend this game.  Most importantly: The gacha component is pretty much only to get your hands on characters! After you got the character you want, it is pretty much like not playing a gacha game anymore! This is important for anyone that feels like "Oohh.. but its a gacha, right?" The whole gacha aspect is only for obtaining the characters! 100%!  After that everything else like weapons, armor, mastery and more has no connection to the whole gacha system! You get to fully embrace the crafting system of the game, which is extremely important and not optional. I usually dislike crafting in MMORPGs, but this one I actually like a lot. This games crafting feels really clean, well organized and with a lot of systemic thought put into it. There are no useless filler items to craft. Everything has a purpose.   The game also won't trivialize the gear you worked so hard for to get with a new patch by simply adding new stuff that everyone gets for free, like WoW or FF14 do for example. Your old stuff stays relevant all the time and is relevant to get new stuff that gets added. Essentially time spend on obtaining and upgrading your things feels like a satisfying investment. Of course in the end you are just playing a game, but within that game it feels like time well spend.  Big recommend for anyone looking for a satisfying daily game to sink time into and build up a nicely curated, managed account collection of characters, equipment and more. Bonus points if you like 7 Deadly Sins obviously.

Overall, this game has a lot of potential and already feels like something special. If you’re a fan of anime-style RPGs or just want a fun open-world game, this is definitely worth trying. Game's pretty decent, I think the bugs being mentioned, technical issues, etc... are being overplayed a bit too much. Don't get me wrong this game's not perfect but I've only really encountered 3 game breaking bugs in my 45 hrs of playing so far. Two with co-op, and one by myself getting stuck somewhere.  It's fun enough for an open world gacha but its not much more than that at this point. Once they add more end-game content and things to do once you get through all the main story and world exploration content it'll (hopefully) feel much more complete and give you an actual purpose to keep playing other than grinding out character upgrades and things.  It is a VERY grindy game at the moment, thats for sure, but with the updates they've announced (continuing to auto-track for materials once you pick up the one that you were tracking cough cough) and finally being able to craft pieces for engraved armor should (again, hopefully) make it feel less grindy. But I do need to admit I have not been able to max out a single character so far despite funneling all of my resources and grinding onto Meliodas. I'm close-ish but still a few days away from maxing him out. Seeing big numbers go boom is nice tho, feels good to see the investment pay off.   Game looks pretty and runs well enough for me, I play on steam deck so I can't expect much but it's been better than I've expected and honestly pleasantly playable on handheld.   Give it a try if you enjoy the 7DS IP and want to experience the new gacha on the block, but keep in mind that things that should have been present from day 1 (cough cough auto-tracking materials cough cough) are still being added. I'll be sticking with the game as it seems that so far the devs are listening to feedback and things seem to be looking up!

Firstly, The game is fun, even addictive, with a bit of luck and enough skill you can easily get to late game after a couple days or a week of playing depending on how much time you invest. I originally thought of it as a genshin clone but the combat system and combat power system feels interesting with a lot of room for possibility and skill expression (though late game you can kinda just face tank and click buttons).   Secondly, My only issue with the game, after getting addicted and dumping nearly a 100 hours over the span of 5 days is that the late game grind feels like an afk simulator, you do a platinum run, buy what you need from shops, craft one pieces of equipment, do 2-5 abyss or hell mode bosses, or some overworld bosses depending on what you need and if you got keys in the mail or if your weekly keys reset. It essentially feels like a mobile afk game where you just get your login bonus, play for a couple minutes then leave which was very different from the early and middle-game. Please add non-cube key ways to get platinum and star fragments for FTP players. FTP isn't that bad though, lots of free stuff.  Lastly, I am completely FTP and I beat the entire story mode and was able to get to nearly 45k combat power pretty easily, it probably helped that I'm good at PvE but nevertheless its not gated behind paid content besides like a good flying mount and if you want to have a game that you can play for a couple hours and watch numbers get bigger and hp bars deplete faster, enjoying team building and making busted combinations with only a bit of time investment every day, its definitely a good pick.  Summary: Decent game 7/10. Add like super hard infinite battle tower that requires skill and like limits your characters as a option to grind plat, cube-keys, and star fragments and maybe the skin voucher thingies and It would be a 9/10. If your not trying to dedicate a bunch of time to a game and just have this on the burner playing every once in a while to optimize what you have its not bad.

[h1]The reviews do not reflect what I have personally experienced in about 100 hours of playtime. [/h1]    Let me start by saying that I think the game is great. So much so that I've found myself logging on every day so far since release.     Whilst I have not personally experienced any bugs except for a minor one that was fixed upon relogging I can't deny that I havent seen some people have some pretty horrible bugs that completely locked them from playing the game. However, the developers have acknowledged them and worked on fixing them.     I like the combat but it could definitely use some polishing. Mainly the tag system that I find myself struggling with sometimes due to it not registering.. Sometimes you have to do a normal attack first for it register which can be quite annoying.     The daily gameplay loop is enjoyable. While you could run around the map for hours on end, collecting resources, you don't have to. You can go for a quick route and getter the most important resources quite quickly. Platinum Ore and bead materials are the most important resource to get daily which honestly doesn't take long at all.     Speaking of gathering.. This is my biggest complaint. The way the current tracking system for resources works is a daily annoyance of mine.. I personally would like to see the developers work on this first. I would like to see a system that shows you the next spot to go to when you track a resource rather than having to go into the menu each and every time for the same resource. Or maybe track all the spots on the map at once and PLEASE make it so it doesn't reset your tracking so easily. If you click on anything else while gathering it just completely stops tracking.   Also the tracking for resources in dungeons or boss challenges rarely if ever works.    The world itself is great, I like it. There aren't very many places on the map that feel low effort.    Weapons could use some more variety in my opinion. I would like to see some more niche weapons that could potentially be BiS with the correct build. Other than that it's been great.    Overall, I think it's a good game that I'll continue to play and I do recommend people to give the game a try!

I went into The Seven Deadly Sins: Origin completely blind. I’ve never watched the anime and honestly that didn’t hurt my experience at all. You can jump right in without any prior knowledge cause the game does a decent job of introducing its world and characters, so newcomers won’t feel lost.  Now let’s get this out of the way: yes, this is a gacha game. No surprises there. If you’re familiar with the genre, you already know what to expect; pulling characters, grinding, and hoping RNGesus is on your side.  At first, I actually had a lot of fun. The open world is enjoyable to explore, and there’s enough going on to keep you engaged… for a while. But after around 20+ hours, I started to feel the fatigue. This is one of those cases where I really wish Steam had a “neutral” review option, because I don’t hate the game, I just don’t feel strongly enough to recommend it either.  It’s pretty clear this game took heavy inspiration from Genshin Impact, to the point where it feels more like a copy than something trying to stand on its own. It follows the same formula almost beat-for-beat, without bringing enough originality to justify itself.  That said, it’s not all bad. The animations are clean and visually appealing, but the gameplay itself feels a bit off. Swapping characters and using abilities can feel slightly delayed and clunky, and even basic movement like sprinting has this awkward delay that makes everything feel less responsive than it should.  One thing I did appreciate is the pacing. Progression feels relatively fast, especially since you can skip cutscenes something I personally enjoyed a lot. It respects your time more than some other games in the genre.  In the end, I don’t see this game attracting a huge player base. This game will appeal to existing fan, but then that audience might not be big enough to keep it thriving.   The gameplay itself isn’t bad… it’s just another gacha game. And in a genre this crowded, “just okay” doesn’t really stand out.

After 4 years of waiting since its announcement, I’ve thoroughly enjoyed this game so far. Sure, I’ve had some frustrating moments with game-breaking bugs, especially the join party bug (fixed btw), but no game launches without bugs. What I appreciate most is that the developers are actively listening to feedback and working to fix issues as quickly as possible with each scheduled maintenance and server patch. Because of that, I believe the game will continue improving if we all work together as a community. Not to mention, there’s so much exciting content ahead when it comes to characters, locations, bosses, you name it. As a big fan of the series, I’m especially excited to see some of my favorite characters appear, like Escanor, Arthur and Ban, along with locations such as Vaizel, Camelot, and Istar.    Of course, everyone has their own opinion on this genre, and I completely understand that. But this game does things differently, and its uniqueness shows through its weapon variety, ultimate combos, dungeons, bosses, and more. When it comes to multiplayer, I love that you can progress alongside your friends like completing quests, fighting bosses, and opening chests together. Honestly, that’s something I always wanted in Genshin Impact.    Right now, the game still has flaws that may turn some people away, but I genuinely recommend giving it a chance. After its recent update, I think we’re heading in the right direction :)

[h1]Open World Gacha Finally Done Right.[/h1]   The Seven Deadly Sins: Origin has become a truly great game that honors its predecessor series very well. Open-world adventure combined with gacha elements, many would rightly say it’s identical to Genshin Impact, but with combat reminiscent of Zenless Zone Zero. Except, we finally have got a great combination of the two positives without the embarrassing overtuned/money-hungry gooner elements. Instead, it delivers a genuinely entertaining story and cool boss fights. There are so many different systems that it would be hard to describe them all in a short review, but aside from the pulls, everything seems farmable within a reasonable time investment. This gem really surprised me in a good way I wouldn’t have expected it to turn out this fantastic and enjoyable. If you’re looking for a new MMORPG to jump into, consider trying it out! I hope they continue in this direction and keep this sentiment of staying non-gooner and focusing on these creative boss mechanics and also, like probably many others, I hope Escanor, Merlin and Ban drops soon as well. 1. Technical Problems  - Connection and server issues: The game has been out for 2 months now, and we still experience moments where we get desynced. - Dodging glitch: Sometimes when trying to dodge, the character just evades on the spot without moving. - Skills failing to cast: Skills either won't activate or get canceled for no reason (specifically Mannie’s skill). - Character swapping delay: There are times when you have to spam the character-switch key, even though the swap should be instantaneous. - Ultimate abilities bug: Certain ultimates cause knocked-down enemies to stand right back up (for example, Daisy’s ultimate forces the dragon back on its feet immediately after use for no reason). - Misleading translations: Several in-game translations need to be reviewed because they are misleading (for instance, King’s Potential 6 text). - Visual clutter in combat: Depending on the characters you use, there are way too many visual effects. It completely clutters the screen and makes it impossible to see what's happening, especially with smaller enemies. - Persistent day-one bugs: There are far too many bugs that have been present since launch (sometimes falling through the map, clipping through walls, etc.) 2. WE NEED REAL CONTENT  - Uninspiring daily/weekly grind: The daily and weekly farm is just boring. Doing 2 bosses/dungeons a day and mining 4 ores isn't engaging, and it doesn't feel rewarding at all. - ZERO CONTENT: We desperately need content, and I mean actual events. Events that just consist of basic missions where you have to spend keys are not fun. Please introduce mini-games, new game modes, etc. (which would also be a great way to earn items, stellar fragments, and so on). - Guild content: Please add content for guilds, such as Guild Bosses or exclusive co-op dungeons. - Renewable PvE modes: Introduce PvE modes that reset periodically, like a Roguelike mode. - Rewards proportional to the grind: The content available should match the amount of farming required. Only 2 "endgame" fights per month is clearly not enough. A better alternative would be a tower that resets every X weeks with different battles. - Dynamic open-world events: Add random events that can trigger while exploring the map, offering small rewards to make exploration more dynamic (for example: running into an NPC being attacked by bandits, etc.).     3. Progression & Farming Problems  - Resource scarcity: There are way too many materials that aren't available in large enough quantities to actually enjoy the game (e.g., Platinum, Sunstones, etc.). - Terrible gear system (worse since v1.3): The equipment system is awful, and it's even worse since version 1.3. If you want this much RNG—to the point where we can end up with an engraving that is worse than before using level/passive upgrade items—then you need to let players farm way more. We are heavily gated on every required item, and we can literally waste those hard-earned resources just to end up with worse stats. - Lack of daily keys: We are starved for daily keys. 50 keys only cover 2 boss challenges or dungeons, which is clearly not enough to actually have fun. - World Boss respawns: Make world bosses respawn without a timer—for example, simply by leaving the area and coming back. - Battle Pass value: The Battle Pass rewards need to be reworked; the current rewards are worth nowhere near the actual price of the pass.   4. Communication Problems  - Post-banner nerfs are unacceptable: Nerfing a character right after their banner ends—once everyone has already pulled for them—is completely unacceptable (Greatsword Escanor). You need to rethink your priorities. It is not normal to leave a bug that affects a new character's performance untouched for 3 weeks, only to "fix" it right after the banner expires when so many people pulled specifically for that performance. - Terrible communication and character previews: You need to showcase characters before they are released. You claim to be against leaks, but the reality is that without leakers, we wouldn't even have all the necessary information before deciding whether to pull. Your communication needs a serious overhaul. It’s the community and the leaks keeping this game alive, not you. You managed to do it properly for Escanor, but for characters like Clotho and Daisy, the communication was at absolute rock bottom. Not to mention the number of times you officially showcase something that has already been live in-game for days or weeks. - Too many original characters: This is a 7DS game. We play a Seven Deadly Sins game for the actual 7DS cast, not for an overload of original characters. 5. Quality of Life  - Detailed loot screen: At the end of a dungeon, display ALL the stats of the dropped gear so we can lock them (or not) right then and there. - Inventory count in descriptions: Show the exact number of an item we currently own whenever we view its item description. - Direct dungeon retry: Allow players to immediately restart a dungeon from the end screen, instead of automatically teleporting them back into the open world. - Legendary capture potion crafting: Give players the ability to craft legendary capture potions, even if it has to be capped with a weekly limit. - On-screen quest tracking: Implement a quest tracker directly on the main screen. - Pinned items for farming: Allow us to pin a specific item somewhere on the screen to track its quantity in real-time without constantly opening the inventory (extremely useful when farming something specific). - Inventory search function: Add a search bar in the inventory/bag. There are way too many items in this game, and it’s sometimes a nightmare to find a specific one. - Weapon-based gear presets: Add gear presets tied directly to the weapons equipped on characters. Since characters use 3 different weapons and require different gear setups for each, having to change everything manually every single time is incredibly tedious. - Pet dispatch/expedition system: Create a feature that allows us to send pets on "expeditions" to passively gather resources like Platinum, Sunstones, flowers, insects, sap, etc. - Dungeon queue transparency: Provide a way to see which party member declined or missed the dungeon queue, so we can easily kick them if they are being completely AKF. - Instant battle retry: Add an option to instantly restart a fight without having to fully exit the instance, whether it's in Space-Time Junction or Boss Challenges. We criticize the game because we care about it. The community wants 7DS Origin to succeed.  Please listen before players lose interest.

A Visual Masterpiece Held Back by Clunky Mechanics (43 Hours In)  Overall Rating: 6/10 (Recommended – with heavy warnings)    I’ve put 43 hours into The Seven Deadly Sins: Origin now, and as a huge fan of the anime, it’s a bittersweet experience. There is so much to love here, but the game is currently being held back by a complete lack of modern Quality of Life (QOL) features.    The Good: A Dream for Anime Fans  Visually Stunning: This game is absolutely beautiful. Britannia looks exactly how I imagined it. From the rolling fields of Liones to the Fairy King’s Forest, the art style is top-tier.    Love for the IP: You can tell the devs love the source material. Playing as Meliodas or Jericho feels impactful, and the combat captures the energy of the series perfectly.    Performance: I’ve run into some bugs, but thankfully nothing game-breaking so far. It’s mostly technical polish that’s missing.    The Bad: The "QOL" Nightmare (Must Read)  While the core gameplay is fun, the friction in the menus and farming systems is honestly exhausting. If the devs want this game to survive long-term, they need to address these issues ASAP:    The Tracking System is Broken: This is my biggest gripe. If I mark a specific material to farm (like a flower), the mark disappears the moment I pick up one item. Why? If I need 10 flowers, I should see all 10 on my map.    Inventory Back-and-Forth: Currently, I have to open my bag over and over again just to see where the next material location is. It’s a waste of time. The markers should stay active continuously until I’ve gathered what I need.    No Material Exchange: We desperately need a material exchange system. I have hundreds of "extra" orbs and stones that I don’t need. Why can't I trade 100 Green Orbs for some Blue Orbs? The lack of a "downgrade/upgrade" or horizontal exchange system makes the grind feel unrewarding and artificially long.    Verdict  If you love 7DS, you will enjoy your time here—the first 20 hours are magical. But once you hit the endgame grind, the "mechanical friction" starts to hurt. We need a huge QOL update to fix the farming methods and material management.    Great game, but the developers need to stop making me look at my inventory and let me look at the beautiful world instead.

I never posted a review before about a game but I feel I need to post on this one. Here it goes:  The game had bugs on release and still has them. What game of this genre didn't? But they are fixing them more and more and rewarding us for it. Cheers for that.  Love the gameplay, team building, character abilities, boss fights and how you can interact with your abilities on the environment.  There's co-op and this is the only game of the genre that allows you to do story mode from start to finish in a party with a max of 5 friends. For me, this is AMAZING.  You can play the game without using a single drop of money. They don't trow promotions on your face every 5 mins. They don't trow them at all.  I love the fact you can actually swim on this game and don't use stamina to stay afloat. Bro this is INSANE. Plus if you dive, they wen't to the trouble to create an entire area of ocean with sea life. Tell me a game of this genre that has that???  In this game you can capture pets to collect stuff for you, mine for you, to use as gliders and to even fly around the map, and have it done since release. No other of this genre has this and if it does for sure didn't had it available since release.  Now my opinion on difficulty:   They were spot on with what they done. It's a game that requires you to understand how a team is supposed to work and how characters interact with each other to provide the desired outcome. Each character has 3 weapons to select from and has to use 1 of them in combat. Some of them turns the character from support into dps or into tank. This one hit the spot for me cuz I came from World of Warcraft and I do love my holy trinity characters ^^ (for more stuff about how the game works,team building, character building etc actually play it, they have the tutorial) You can craft your gear and they offer you on an event the materials necessary to craft your first set of gear for 1 of the characters and his/her weapon for free. Rly good on them.  You understand the concept of team and character building you'll have an amazing time getting to endgame.  You don't understand it, try and read a bit more about it or ask ppl that understand to help you and for sure you will get it. If you don't, your time doing endgame won't be fun. But that's endgame for you. Plus this is the reason for most of the negative reviews, ppl that even after 130+ something hours in the game, didn't understand the concept, were carried in most of the stuff and end up complaining about Gray Demon difficulty in World 4 etc.....  This game has a lot of love from streamers on Twitch and Youtube so if you have any difficulty understanding something just Google about the game or find videos about it.   I uninstalled all the games I had on my computer, leaving only Yu-Gi-Oh Duel Links, that's how much I'm enjoying this game. I have 285+ hours on steam plus a few more on mobile. Ya I had bugs but most of them were rly funny and me and my friends laughed a lot cuz of them. In the end for me it's a 8.5/10 the -1.5 is from Tristan not being Holy/Dark. Why???  Have fun guys and don't forget to hydrate xD   P.S. I'm Portuguese main language so sorry if format or some words on phrase construction are not correct. I tried my best.  If my best wasn't enough here's a song for you, it belongs to Coldplay and it's called Fix You: "If you try your best, but you don't succed..."  :D  For the DEV's: keep up the good work and do something about your English text translation team ^^ Oh and if possible, I don't know if its a bug or not but Hell mode could be 15 cube keys instead of 20 since Abyss is 20 (and pls keep Abyss at 20 and not more)   I'ma right guys?

The Seven Deadly Sins: Origin is a good game. The fun outweighs the suck.   I like the story, the interactions, the graphics, the voice acting, and the music. And I think this game would be even better minus all the multiplayer filler. Designers often design MMORPGs to suck in order to encourage players to play together, so that the game sucks less. I think we should all strive for a higher standard, otherwise video games will become a passing fad. It's bad enough when we accidentally add suck to our games, but it is nearly unforgivable when we intentionally add suck to our games. The mission of all game developers and game designers should be to create consistently fun interactive fiction; the mission should never be to create games that suck.   But my thoughts on the state of multiplayer gaming aside, 7ds Origin raises the standard for multiplayer games. It puts the story first, not filler first. And the story is phenomenal. It's basically a brand new season of The Seven Deadly Sins. Who needs to stream episodes when we can just play it in a video game? I like that.  The Seven Deadly Sins is a multiplayer game that features a great story. In spite of the suck, the game is a lot of fun.

After over 150 hours into this game. i'd say it certinly has some issues, alot were addressed in recent patches, it certinly needs more work, but it is enjoyable, the combat is fun, with the new additions of abyss dungeons, it can be quite challenging at times, co-op/multiplayer is also quite fun. overall i'd give the game a 7/10.  it probably should have stayed in beta a while longer to address alot of the regular play problems. there is a fair amount of crashing when it comes to playing on console and mobile, but for the most part the PC version seems pretty stable.    Is it worth spending money on?:  50/50 there are some cost/time effective purchases, but id say the comparison between my free account, and my paid purchases accounts are negligable, the most noticable diffrence is in obtaining gear via purchaseing keys, and the duplicates of SSR units. otherwise they can both complete the same content, all-be with a bit more effort require on the free account due to gearing diffrences. so i'd say your fine either way, atleast for the time being. this may be subject to change in the future.    Gameplay:  Overall good, crashes can be annoying, falling through the world has its own complications usually requireing the use of a trasnlator and contacting the korean support to get fixed, for some reason the global support seems to be sitting on their hands.  otherwise the visuals are rather good, the world in general is visually appealing, combat visuals can be an issue when spamming skills, and the camera orientation can be one of the largest frustrations, sometimes being entirely blocked when near walls in combat.    Closing:  I hope to see this game expand, and address more of the common issues in coming patches, its certinly worth playing, and an enjoyable experiance on PC as theres less crashing, and general issues. if your looking for somthing fun, enjoy the Seven Deadly Sins, are looking for somthing that doesnt require a lot of daily dedication and your willing to see how it expands and grows, this might be the kinda game your after.

I'm not even sure where to begin. The Seven Deadly Sins IP is my roman empire, and when I first heard of this game's release I was absolutely thrilled. I'm well versed in how gacha games function, as I have four years worth of game time in Genshin Impact, have been playing Honkai Star Rail since its release, and have dabbled in both Wuthering Waves and Honkai Impact 3. So, with that context, let's break down 7DS Origin.   The story is wonderful. I only have so much time in my day so I haven't completed it all the way through to act 12, but the plot devices they use to let you explore Britannia and encounter your favorite characters while making it a fresh narrative experience was way better than I ever imagined. I don't even want to spoil anything that happens, if you have the time I implore you to play it yourself.  You'll see a lot in these reviews that the key economy, (the in game resource that allows you to make your characters more powerful), is incredibly limited. What you have to consider is that gacha games like Origin are not meant to be played for more than a few hours a day at most once you reach mid/late game. Even then, the game affords that to you, as it is incredibly generous with free keys that it gives you daily, as well as keys you can purchase with your excess star trace fragments, i.e. the free secondary resource you get every time you pull on a banner. I've fought with the Genshin resin system, and the way Origin handles it feels like a dream.  Secondly, this is an open world gacha game. This means the free currency to pull is limited to first encounters with the world and the events that come out. Like with any gacha game, this currency is incredibly generous at the beginning. I've gotten enough of it to go to pity four times on the standard banner and once on the first limited banner. Compared to other games I've played, it's literally just fine. It's not groundbreaking, but it's standard for literally any other gacha game I've played. Anyone complaining on that front just doesn't understand how games like these function, especially considering they are free games that have to fund themselves somehow.  There's also a lot of complaints in these reviews when it comes to the amount of times you have to pull a character to unlock their full potential, but they don't really mention how half of those potential points are simply given to you for leveling up their stats. Also, it's a gacha game. You're gonna have to pull several copies for them to be more powerful. That's how a gacha works. Meliodas is fully functional with the free potential points you get for simply investing in his weapons.  I have to clarify though this is one buggy, buggy game. [SPOT WHERE I'VE EDITED MY REVIEW AS THE GAME HAS UPDATED!] [EDITED AGAIN AS BUG FIXES COME OUT!] I've yet to encounter any game breaking bugs as of version 1.2! Bravo dev team!  The co-op system is a dream. It flows absolutely seamlessly, allowing you to have your own full party of characters to fight with alongside the four other characters each person you're playing with has. Puzzles, chests, and even the story itself can be played flawlessly with assistance from friends or just a friendly stranger, which is especially important since it also allows you to have help with story related boss fights if you're struggling with them. This is personally my favorite aspect of the game, and it outshines nearly every other gacha co-op experience I've ever had.  The combat system I've found is a slightly alarming lovechild between Elden Ring and Wuthering Waves. As there are no proper sustain units in this game (yet), you have to go out of your way to experience and memorize the attack patterns of bosses and simply get good. It can be incredibly frustrating if you're not a fan of a souls' like approach, but the incredibly flexible team comps you can put together make up for it in my opinion. The auto-aiming function of the combat system is honestly a little janky, as well as bosses just kind of meandering outside of their arena and resetting themselves.   [EDITED SPOT AS THE GAME EXPERIENCES MORE BUG FIXES!] Resource gathering USED to be a pain in the neck, but the resource tracking system and all of my complaints with it have been fixed! It flows utterly seamlessly now, and the only babygate is that it physically takes time to pick up all of the resources you need! Again, bravo dev team.  At the end of the day, it's a Seven Deadly Sins gacha game. If you enjoy 7DS, and you enjoy a grindy gacha game, this is the game for you. If either of those things aren't what you typically enjoy, you're not going to enjoy this game. It's a game for a pre-established franchise that lets you explore a world that you wouldn't be able to experience to this level otherwise. It's a little buggy, but that's honestly the only real drawback it has, and bugs can be patched.   If you like 7DS, absolutely play this game. If you don't, it's a free game; just give it a few hours of your time and uninstall it if it isn't your vibe. With a full and very responsive dev team, the drawbacks of this game are going to be worked on in no time, so I will absolutely suggest this game with my full chest.  END OF POST EDIT! I am absolutely elated to share with the class that Seven Deadly Sins Origin is EXTREMELY responsive when it comes to quality of life updates and general bug fixes, and is VERY generous when it comes to compensating for said QoL updates! It's a breath of fresh air when it comes to gacha game customer service and I am absolutely blown away by this approach!

Although this game is still very new, which means it gets some wiggle room with day 1 bugs and issues in my book, there are some things that have been completely oversighted and ignored by the developers that have yet to be addressed or updated.  I have many hours in this game and to be truthful, I enjoy it a lot. I haven't even watched 7ds or any related media to it, and yet, I love the character designs and the attention to detail (the game on anything BUT very-low graphics looks STUNNING). However, the amount of issues in this game completely outweigh the pretty colors and the maybe-AI generated art used for various materials (ESPECIALLY THE FOOD, it might just be the art style, but it looks so AI to me).  First of all, **THERE IS NO POULTRY.** I cannot stress this enough and how much it bothers me that a frequently used ingredient for SO MANY recipes is completely unobtainable in game, and if it isn't, WHY IS IT SO DIFFICULT TO GET?? No one knows how to get it, none of the bird creatures drop it, chickens are classified under the "animals" tab, meaning they die in one hit and drop nothing (so useless btw??), sooo? There are already so many recipes that REQUIRED poultry as an ingredient, so why add all of these recipes and somehow forget to add the main ingredient? I hope that they eventually listen to the confused players who have been googling and stalking the multiple wiki's for "how to get poultry in 7ds:o", and add a function where the Big Baby Birds and/or Storm Birds drop poultry on death, so that it can be obtained early-game.   Second, the map system. Markers frequently break and turn into white boxes that PERMANENTLY stay there (I personally have 5 or 6 on my map), you can't checkmark pins of Book of Stars puzzles that you've already finished, map regions don't unlock when you've entered the region, but only unlock if you activate a warp point IN said region, map zoom in is limited, theres a lot more but it just get's more nitpicky from here if anything. The white square bothers me the most, because it has yet to be fixed.  Third, multiplayer. If you are placed into the America West servers, you can't play with people in America East servers, which makes online multiplayer super limited. A friend of mine who lives in the East had to create a brand new game after progressing really far, just to play on our servers. Party features also constantly break, a day one issue was where if you failed to join a party, you'd be placed on a white box below the map, and although they supposedly fixed this bug, it still happens. Even more bugs have occurred actually, when attempting to join some ones party, it typically takes 3-4 tries until a successful join. Cutscenes frequently break or go out of sync in multiplayer, gliders sometimes break and duplicate of other players, the multiplayer system is just generally broken.   Although I have been ragging on this game this entire review, but I like playing it, and I recommend playing it, buttt maybe later down the line when more things have been improved upon. It's difficult as new player to constantly be needing to reference the may wiki's (I do highly recommend zeroluck.gg though, the person behind the wiki is super helpful and is doing an amazing job) , and needing to constantly google search questions because of so much confusion. I really hope we get bigger updates to the game soon that improve all of these issues, because I genuinely haven't been so frustrated at game bugs before in my life lol. If this hurts your feelings I am NOT sorry.... Ether talking about you at this point since this review triggered you so much.  First off, people need to remember what this actually is. A free-to-play game, NOT a $70 AAA title with a fully finished campaign on day one. The story isn’t even complete yet, and it’s clearly being built out over time. And let’s be real, even a lot of $70 AAA games don’t launch with a complete story anymore. You end up buying 2–3 DLCs at $20–$60 each just to finish it (You know who you are SMH).  I’m about 60 hours in (I feel like I have no lifed this thing). I finished the main story and most side content around 40 hours, and by that point you’re sitting at around 50 free keys a day. That’s enough for roughly 5 fully rewarded dungeon or boss runs daily. After that, you can still run those activities endlessly without rewards to help friends (that is if you had any), plus you and I both know YOU haven't 100% the game still, you're just trying to be OP right now. So the idea that you “can’t play” after using keys just isn’t true.  Are there limits? Sure, but your mom has limits too. So unless you’re trying to dump 5–6 hours into the game every single day, there’s more than enough to do between your daily runs, exploration, build progression, and helping others. If you’re blowing through everything and still complaining there’s nothing to do… that’s more of a pacing issue (that's what your ex told me) than a game design failure. Maybe go touch some grass?  The grind complaints are also overblown. Progression slows down after the early game, which is intentional. You’re not supposed to be doing 20–40 pulls every day forever. Over time, resources stack up and it becomes about consistency, not instant gratification. (Not everything is about you getting yours)  And the “pay-to-play” argument doesn’t really hold up either. This is a gacha game, sure, but there’s no PvP, so there’s no whale ruining your experience or beating you every time you log in. Nobody is forcing you to spend money to keep up with anyone else. You can play completely at your own pace. So put down moms credit card, or just buy the stuff you want and say your sister did it.  Boss resets and movement issues do exist, but once you understand positioning and how fights work, it becomes manageable and part of the learning curve, not some constant game-breaking problem. The other solution is just be better and kill the boss faster...  Now to be fair, bugs and performance issues are the biggest real concern right now, and those absolutely deserve attention. Just like your bad attitude about this game could use some attention... Really though they aren't that bad, Madden is 70 bucks and people still buy that (the most bugged game ever)  Where this game really stands out, and even critics admit it, is the world and overall feel. It captures its style extremely well, and when everything clicks, it’s genuinely fun to play.  At the end of the day, this isn’t a game you rush through and finish in a weekend. It’s meant to be played over time. If you go in expecting a fully finished AAA experience with nonstop rewards, you’re going to be disappointed. If you understand what it is, there’s a lot here to enjoy.   It’s not perfect.... yet, but it’s far from the “dead game” people are trying to make it out to be.

Take this review from a player that's quit every gacha he's played so far.     This game is good, not "amazing", not "the best", it's good. I'd say the system is f2p friendly, you get a decent amount of pulls, and for the first event banner when you pull 120 times you get a gurantee separate from pity. And as of writing this review I've gotten well over 200+ EVENT banner pulls.     Best thing about the game system is the weapons/crafting system. You don't have to pull for ssr/5 star weapons, even the event/signature weapon—you can craft them, and it's decently easy too.\    For weapon tier ups/constilations you don't need a dupe of that specific weapon. It's kind of like a separate level up system but for weapon passives. You just need to scrap a couple of the same tier weapon to get tier up stones, some peeps already have the event weapon to tier 5 while being f2p, me? I'm not that grindy.    For character dupe passives it goes up to 10 but you get 4 dupes for free from mastery. Some characters do suck, like King (dps) (his support is decent though). But each character has 3 weapon types/kits they can use, each of them can be different in terms of element or even role (dps, support, healer) but you only use one of the variants of course, you can switch freely though.    Each character also has an adventure skill, these are out of combat skills that make clearing the map easier, these can range from movement skills, running on walls, becoming tarzan, or literally flying up to a mountain, to more of an interactive map skills, displaying nearby chest on the minimap, materials, etc...    Overall this game is solid for an early release, there is still a lot of content missing, like the hawk pass, basically a battlepass, and there are a decent amount of bugs like permadeath. But the most annoying part of this game is the stupid bosses. They're not technically hard like impossible to kill, most bosses are really squishy when you build your characters, the annoying thing is, they spam a lot, like giving a 5 year old a controller that only does special moves. But there is still so much potential, especially when it comes to future characters.

Would I recommend this game in it's current version? I would since for the most part it can be completed by a casual player so long as they take advantage of the keys daily. The Seven Deadly Sins: Origin is a free‑to‑play open‑world action RPG developed and published by Netmarble F&C, set in the anime universe of The Seven Deadly Sins. Released on March 16, 2026, on PC (Steam), PS5, and mobile platforms, the game combines exploration, combat, and story‑driven quests in a vast reimagined Britannia.      In the game, you take on the role of Prince Tristan of Liones as the world is thrown into chaos by a mysterious collision of time and space. The campaign features a brand‑new original story alongside familiar characters from the series. Explore open landscapes full of secrets, monsters, hidden dungeons, and dynamic activities like fishing, cooking, and crafting.      Combat is real‑time and action‑oriented, where you build a team of heroes with different abilities and switch between them during battle. You’ll encounter powerful enemies, combo‑based fights, and co‑op gameplay with others. The game also mixes life‑skill content such as exploration and resource gathering with RPG progression systems.      Overall, The Seven Deadly Sins: Origin delivers a large open‑world anime experience with rich visuals and a mix of action and exploration. While it’s ambitious and offers multiplayer features, some players have noted mixed reactions due to performance, optimization, and online aspects.

the Seven Deadly Sins: Origin is an incredible game with a breathtaking open world and a combat system that feels truly satisfying. As a fan, it’s everything I wanted—the visuals are stunning and the gameplay is top-notch. However, the experience is currently held back by numerous technical glitches. While the performance is stable (no major FPS drops), I encountered frequent bugs that interfere with the flow of the game, from character clipping to quest-breaking glitches. It’s a "hidden gem" that desperately needs a few more patches to be perfect.  Pros:  Faithful and beautiful anime-style graphics.  Smooth performance with no frame drops.  Deep and engaging exploration.  Cons:  Frequent bugs and glitches.  Some mechanics feel "unpolished" at the moment.  Final Verdict: The game itself is fantastic, but the technical bugs can be frustrating. If you can handle a few glitches while waiting for updates, it's definitely worth playing.

When the gaming community has been oversaturated with open-world anime gacha games, here comes probably one of the most controversial open world gacha games to date that nobody asked for, also using one of the most controversial anime IPs as well. I have been following SDS for a very long time, and I have played netmarble's other title SDS: Grand Cross since it's JP release years ago. I did play SDS Origins during the closed beta and I want to give my honest review since then and compared to the full launch.    The game isn't perfect, but that doesn't mean it can't be better or improved. Since the closed beta, Netmarble has done a good job highlighting issues and adding more mechanics, as well as communicating them with their players. The gameplay isn't necessarily as challenging or innovative as other titles such as Wuthering Waves or Punishing Gray Raven, however it is addictive and team up ults are a nice feature. The open world is beautiful and they really captured the land of Britannia perfectly compared to the anime. The story in the beginning few chapters can feel pretty slow as the game takes place after the end of the original manga. However, seeing fan favorite characters, and how Netmarble has worked with Nakaba to expand the story has been excellent. Every character in the game can use three different weapons and there are different animations for each weapon, each one changing the playstyle of the character. The game rewards exploration and crafting to level up your workbench and there are plenty of F2P ways to earn SSR or 5 Star Weapons for your characters. Like other gacha games, there is a pity system but it is not as expensive or time consuming as other games and they don't punish you for not getting the character first try. I wish the game had english voice acting but the JP and KR VAs have done a great job capturing the characters and keeping the game immersive, cutscenes are beautifully animated and the boss fights in this game really immerse you in the world of Britannia.     With that being said, there are few optimization issues apparent, while I did not fall victim to any of them, some players may experience issues. Overall however, I do believe this is a game everyone should at least try and go into with no bias as Netmarble has really outdone themselves with this title. You don't have to be a long time SDS fan to enjoy this game and I think this title has the potential to really go the distance as long as Netmarble continues to communicate with their players and do right by the title.

I love the story from the manga & the anime, I was really looking forward to enjoying this game but unfortunately after the most recent update, I will be uninstalling the game. If you are looking for a Seven Deadly Sins game, I highly recommend Seven Deadly Sins: Grand cross. It isn't open world, however, it is a card turn-based gacha game that is loved by the devs, and they listen to their community.   If you like the story of Seven Deadly Sins play Grand cross. If you enjoy an endless grind and want to constantly be farming for gear then play Origin. I've played many games of this style (Genshin Impact, Honkai Impact 3rd, Honkai Star Rail, Wuthering Waves, NTE, ZZZ, and ToF.) This game just needs to much love and they need to start listening to their community if they want to succeed. The recent gear changes were a huge change that made most of my non-engraved gear go to waste. They also will not add Meliodas to the standard banner. He is a limited time unit, and the banner lasts for 21 days once you activate your account. They also kinda listened to us by allowing us to pick up Daisy, however, they chose to do this when they announced Merlin as a new character. As someone who likes The Seven Deadly Sins more than I probably should, this game has been a huge disappointment. I never really expected it to be all that great, but I was hopeful to be proven wrong. It's basically just Genshin Impact or Wuthering Waves with a 7DS skin on top. The 7DS IP is the only thing going for the game, since it doesn't really do anything better than the previously mentioned games.  As someone who does tend to spend more money than I should on games like these, your money is better spent elsewhere. The characters feel very underwhelming and don't deserve to cost as much as they potentially may cost to acquire them.  A good example of this is Escanor. He's pretty much the most popular character from this IP as well as being one of the strongest. After using him for a bit and leveling him up as much as possible, he doesn't feel good at all. Granted, I didn't completely max out all of his skills and get perfect equipment and weapons, I feel like he should still be doing way more damage than he is.  Even if he wasn't a disappointment, all of the characters have this very cheap feel to them. Nothing you do really feels great in terms of feedback. You could be doing big damage to someone and it just doesn't feel satisfying like it does in other similar games.   The biggest issue the game has though, is the absolute grind that is built into everything. Characters require so many resources to fully level up, and getting those resources is an absolute slog. The game has a stamina system in the form of keys which is pretty normal for this type of game, but you just don't get enough. At the point I'm at, I have about 50 or 60 keys max a day. That might sound like a lot but everything in the game takes 5 to 10 keys to claim.  Most of the enemies and bosses only drop a couple of their particular item at a time. This means you have to fight the enemy over and over, which is made worse by the fact that they sometimes take upwards of 20 to 30 minutes to respawn as far as I've seen.  Stuff like armor is similar but even worse. You could do the same fight 5 times and not get the singular piece of gear you need to craft the whole set, which means you either need to buy more keys or just wait a day. At the beginning it can be a bit fun because you don't need too much stuff and gear doesn't really matter, but once you're actually trying to build you character for end game stuff it gets to be too much.   The devs have shown to care more than I would expect, and they do seem to listen to the community. This is a very good thing compared to some games where the devs just don't care at all, but the changes are still slow. The game could be in a much better state in a year or so from now (assuming it's still alive), but I don't want to force myself to stick around until that happens.   In conclusion, I don't recommend this game. Even as someone who really likes certain aspects of The Seven Deadly Sins, this game just doesn't do enough to make me care. Opening it every day to do the same things over and over and maybe adding a few percentages to a characters' stats just isn't fun. Your money is much better spent on anything other than a gacha game, but if you must play one, this isn't it.

I’ve been playing this game for over a month since launch, and it honestly went from [i] “this is my new obsession” [/i] to [i] “why am I clocking in for daily quests like it’s a shift job?” [/i]  At the start, it was incredible. The world is gorgeous, animations are smooth, combat feels fun, and exploration actually had that wow factor. I was fully locked in and excited every time I logged in.  But after the honeymoon phase, the loop became very clear: dailies, grind materials, repeat… and try not to think about the main story too much or I might actually finish it and face reality.  Crafting deserves its own apology letter. It doesn’t feel like crafting anymore—it feels like the game handing you a checklist and saying [i] “suffer a little first.” [/i]  Progression also starts to slow down hard, and it becomes very noticeable how much easier things feel if you’re willing to spend money. Add in occasional lag and performance issues, and the excitement slowly starts to fade.  The funny thing is… I still like it. That’s the problem. The foundation is amazing. The world, visuals, and animation are genuinely top-tier. But the longer you play, the more it shifts from an epic adventure into a repetitive daily routine with anime graphics.  I’ve gone from [i] “wow this is amazing” [/i] to [i] “okay let me just do my chores in this fantasy world real quick.” [/i]  [h3] 6/10 — incredible potential, but the grind loop and progression systems really drain the fun over time.  [/h3]

this game has a lot of problems in late game. slog without character dupes but they don't give enough free currency to even think of pulling dupes. boring daily grind loop run all around the map picking flowers and mining basically. only enough daily energy to do 2 bosses which have crap rates.    Visually its good and I think it could be good but they need to do some actual improvements.

1. Technical Problems  - Connection and server issues: The game has been out for 2 months now, and we still experience moments where we get desynced. - Dodging glitch: Sometimes when trying to dodge, the character just evades on the spot without moving. - Skills failing to cast: Skills either won't activate or get canceled for no reason (specifically Mannie’s skill). - Character swapping delay: There are times when you have to spam the character-switch key, even though the swap should be instantaneous. - Ultimate abilities bug: Certain ultimates cause knocked-down enemies to stand right back up (for example, Daisy’s ultimate forces the dragon back on its feet immediately after use for no reason). - Misleading translations: Several in-game translations need to be reviewed because they are misleading (for instance, King’s Potential 6 text). - Visual clutter in combat: Depending on the characters you use, there are way too many visual effects. It completely clutters the screen and makes it impossible to see what's happening, especially with smaller enemies. - Persistent day-one bugs: There are far too many bugs that have been present since launch (sometimes falling through the map, clipping through walls, etc.) 2. WE NEED REAL CONTENT  - Uninspiring daily/weekly grind: The daily and weekly farm is just boring. Doing 2 bosses/dungeons a day and mining 4 ores isn't engaging, and it doesn't feel rewarding at all. - ZERO CONTENT: We desperately need content, and I mean actual events. Events that just consist of basic missions where you have to spend keys are not fun. Please introduce mini-games, new game modes, etc. (which would also be a great way to earn items, stellar fragments, and so on). - Guild content: Please add content for guilds, such as Guild Bosses or exclusive co-op dungeons. - Renewable PvE modes: Introduce PvE modes that reset periodically, like a Roguelike mode. - Rewards proportional to the grind: The content available should match the amount of farming required. Only 2 "endgame" fights per month is clearly not enough. A better alternative would be a tower that resets every X weeks with different battles. - Dynamic open-world events: Add random events that can trigger while exploring the map, offering small rewards to make exploration more dynamic (for example: running into an NPC being attacked by bandits, etc.).     3. Progression & Farming Problems  - Resource scarcity: There are way too many materials that aren't available in large enough quantities to actually enjoy the game (e.g., Platinum, Sunstones, etc.). - Terrible gear system (worse since v1.3): The equipment system is awful, and it's even worse since version 1.3. If you want this much RNG—to the point where we can end up with an engraving that is worse than before using level/passive upgrade items—then you need to let players farm way more. We are heavily gated on every required item, and we can literally waste those hard-earned resources just to end up with worse stats. - Lack of daily keys: We are starved for daily keys. 50 keys only cover 2 boss challenges or dungeons, which is clearly not enough to actually have fun. - World Boss respawns: Make world bosses respawn without a timer—for example, simply by leaving the area and coming back. - Battle Pass value: The Battle Pass rewards need to be reworked; the current rewards are worth nowhere near the actual price of the pass.   4. Communication Problems  - Post-banner nerfs are unacceptable: Nerfing a character right after their banner ends—once everyone has already pulled for them—is completely unacceptable (Greatsword Escanor). You need to rethink your priorities. It is not normal to leave a bug that affects a new character's performance untouched for 3 weeks, only to "fix" it right after the banner expires when so many people pulled specifically for that performance. - Terrible communication and character previews: You need to showcase characters before they are released. You claim to be against leaks, but the reality is that without leakers, we wouldn't even have all the necessary information before deciding whether to pull. Your communication needs a serious overhaul. It’s the community and the leaks keeping this game alive, not you. You managed to do it properly for Escanor, but for characters like Clotho and Daisy, the communication was at absolute rock bottom. Not to mention the number of times you officially showcase something that has already been live in-game for days or weeks. - Too many original characters: This is a 7DS game. We play a Seven Deadly Sins game for the actual 7DS cast, not for an overload of original characters. 5. Quality of Life  - Detailed loot screen: At the end of a dungeon, display ALL the stats of the dropped gear so we can lock them (or not) right then and there. - Inventory count in descriptions: Show the exact number of an item we currently own whenever we view its item description. - Direct dungeon retry: Allow players to immediately restart a dungeon from the end screen, instead of automatically teleporting them back into the open world. - Legendary capture potion crafting: Give players the ability to craft legendary capture potions, even if it has to be capped with a weekly limit. - On-screen quest tracking: Implement a quest tracker directly on the main screen. - Pinned items for farming: Allow us to pin a specific item somewhere on the screen to track its quantity in real-time without constantly opening the inventory (extremely useful when farming something specific). - Inventory search function: Add a search bar in the inventory/bag. There are way too many items in this game, and it’s sometimes a nightmare to find a specific one. - Weapon-based gear presets: Add gear presets tied directly to the weapons equipped on characters. Since characters use 3 different weapons and require different gear setups for each, having to change everything manually every single time is incredibly tedious. - Pet dispatch/expedition system: Create a feature that allows us to send pets on "expeditions" to passively gather resources like Platinum, Sunstones, flowers, insects, sap, etc. - Dungeon queue transparency: Provide a way to see which party member declined or missed the dungeon queue, so we can easily kick them if they are being completely AKF. - Instant battle retry: Add an option to instantly restart a fight without having to fully exit the instance, whether it's in Space-Time Junction or Boss Challenges. We criticize the game because we care about it. The community wants 7DS Origin to succeed.  Please listen before players lose interest.

My overall experience with the game has been mostly positive until it comes to progression systems, dungeon grinding, and boss dungeon requirements. One of the biggest issues is the reward structure. I constantly receive large amounts of lower-tier drops instead of the materials actually needed for progression. A crafting or conversion system that allows lower-tier materials to be upgraded into higher-tier progression resources would greatly improve the experience.  At this point, I would have to give the game a 2-star review. It is honestly surprising that after so many gacha games over the years, the developers still seem to have learned very little from previous progression and RNG-related issues. The game heavily relies on RNG systems, but newer or lower-rank players who are unfamiliar with this type of progression design can quickly become frustrated. In my case, the Fraudrin encounter became difficult enough that I had to ask for multiplayer assistance, which is something I rarely do in games.  Performance is also a major issue. My PC is a high-end gaming setup with 64 GB of RAM running on medium settings, yet the game still struggles with stability and optimization.  There are also several gameplay problems that negatively impact combat. For example, when swapping characters, the game automatically locks onto targets. In dungeons such as Guila’s, this becomes extremely frustrating because changing characters can instantly force the camera and targeting system onto the enemy, causing unavoidable hits.   Another major issue is the dungeon key and reward system. Some players are able to obtain the materials they need quickly, while others are forced to spend 10–20 keys, and in some cases even 50–60 keys, just to obtain a single required drop. Having progression tied this heavily to inconsistent RNG creates a frustrating and unrewarding experience, especially when players can spend large amounts of time and resources with little to no meaningful progress.  The game also feels aggressively time-gated. In addition, there are numerous bugs currently affecting players, including:  [*] players getting stuck in Friezen Mine (or under the mines) [*] death bugs preventing progression and bricking accounts [*] characters falling underneath the map with no way to escape [*] overall instability and broken systems  At this rate, I stopped focusing on doing the story now due to how bad the RNG is and just wandering around looking for friends at this rate. I'm only playing this game now for 3 things:  [*] Map experience aka exploration, [*] making friends or attempting too,  [*] just playing to nab the seven deadly sins characters like a rare species of animals.  recommendation(s):  [*] Add better craft able material for tier ups [*] optimize some of the dungeons  [*] maybe a better way for exploration for chests or Star fragments  The world itself is enjoyable, and there is clear potential here, but the progression systems, optimization issues, bugs, and heavy RNG dependency significantly hurt the overall experience. If you want to try a game out, and your a big fan of anime, I wouldn't recommend this game until they fix some of the bugs.💛

Seven Deadly Sins: Origin shows obvious progress in terms of stability. The problems that plagued previous versions have been largely addressed, the performance feels smoother, and the game itself seems technically improved compared to its predecessors. In purely practical terms, this game seems to have more going for it. However, even in terms of stability, the problem of engagement arises, which makes the gaming experience less appealing.    The main advantage of this game can be identified in the beginning of the gameplay. It is fun, easy, and thrilling. Progression goes well, there are impressive fights, and the process of gathering your team makes it seem interesting and exciting. At some point, the game actually seems to have a lot of potential.    However, as soon as one gets into the endgame part of the gameplay, everything changes dramatically. The progression starts slowing down and becomes much more boring and repetitive. There are no new mechanics to introduce and learn, only grinding of the same things over and over again. The number of choices made throughout the game becomes more limited.    There is another issue: the restrictive number of cube keys that are obtained each day. It is possible to obtain up to 50 of those per day. While it is fine when it comes to obtaining drops from bosses, at some point the game turns into a slow and painful grind that has nothing new about it. Boss drops are also very RNG based, which means that you will probably need to defeat the same enemy many times before obtaining a certain drop due to the daily limit.    This issue is further complicated by the problem with the boss battles. As there are too many abilities and animations used at once during fights, it can sometimes be hard to see the boss clearly. Sometimes during more complex and fast-paced fights, you can even miss a mechanic because there is simply too much happening at once. Instead of giving the player an opportunity to think tactically, some battles turn out chaotic.    There is also the problem with the inability to reset either your characters or your whole team. If the player invested enough time and money to build their team around a certain hero like Meliodas, then changing their strategy will not be very easy. Therefore, a lot of experiments are discouraged. The ability to easily switch builds will open doors for more creativity and variety    Overall, the game feels quite underwhelming in its current state. There is a solid foundation and the beginning experience shows promise, but the end game design holds it back. Between grinding repetition, RNG dependent rewards, cubekey limitations, visual clutter in boss fights and limited team flexibility, the long term experience lacks depth. It starts strong but is doesnt sustain its momentum making the overal experience feel shallow(?).
