The Seven Deadly Sins: Origin.....  Honestly this game is weird.  Like it’s fun. Really fun at first. Running around the world, flashy combat, giant effects everywhere, random chaos happening every 3 seconds. Brain sees colours. Brain happy.  Then the game suddenly remembers it’s free to play and decides your enjoyment has gone on far to long. Like WTF!  you’re killing stuff fine. Next minute bosses are two-shotting you so hard your ancestors feel it. So naturally you think: “Alright, guess I need better gear.”  How do I get better gear?  Apparently by already having better gear.  Brilliant system. Absolutely incredible. ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ job.  So now you get stuck in that lovely free-to-play loop where everything starts feeling artificially slower. Upgrade materials vanish, enemies become sponges, and every menu starts looking at your wallet like a child staring through a Sweetshop window.  And the brightness in this game needs to calm the ♥♥♥♥ down. Every attack looks like someone fired a rainbow directly into my eyegaballs. Half the time I can’t even tell what killed me because the screen looks like a transformer exploded inside a fireworks factory.  And that’s the annoying part.  Because buried underneath all the give me your money crackhead energy is a genuinely fun game trying to escape captivity.  It has a good concept. Combat’s fun. The world’s decent. Running around with the characters actually feels good for a while.  But after about 30 hours I just stopped playing.  Not because I hated it. Because it started feeling less like progression and more like the game standing outside my house shaking a donation bucket.  Without proper balancing this game’s just going to sit there while people slowly stop playing it because eventually it stops feeling rewarding and starts feeling like a hostage negotiation.  Right. Until the total daily key limit is increased to at least 150 and the platinum economy is fixed this game will remain fundamentally negative in its progression design.    It’s honestly shocking that after so many gacha games have come and gone, the developers seem to have learned absolutely nothing from them.    Right now, the entire game revolves around RNG. That would be fine if players could actually farm. But you can’t farm when you’re hard‑capped at 50 keys per day, the very resource required to claim rewards from dungeons and bosses.    Abyss Dungeon: 20 keys per run    Result: You get two runs per day. That’s it.    The game is aggressively time‑gated.    And then there’s platinum. You need it for everything:    Outfit engravings cost 25 platinum ingots, yet you can only earn 12 per day.    SSR weapons require 16 platinum plus key‑gated materials.    Hero‑specific SSR weapons? 30–40 platinum plus more materials.    Mastery nodes? Some materials are daily‑limited, others require keys. Node IV alone can’t even be completed with the full 50‑key daily cap.    The ratios make no sense. None.    They even removed mastery drops from world mobs, as if that was some groundbreaking improvement. They added a “reset magic gauge” mechanic that just resets your ult when entering a dungeon—pointless and boring. They removed the fast mount jump “for mobile performance,” and since that patch the world looks worse: objects pop in and out, bushes appear and disappear two steps away, and the rendering feels broken.    And then there’s co‑op.  How are players supposed to enjoy co‑op when the entire game is locked behind a 50‑key daily limit?    It’s unreasonable.    The monetisation doesn’t help . Skins? Minimum 60£.  If you don’t pay, you hit a wall fast. And for anyone saying “just buy key bundles,” that argument doesn’t deserve a polite response.    The list goes on.    The game is fun ,until you finish the early content, and what is left is upgrading heroes and farm, eventually you will  run out of the one‑time rewards. After that, the real grind begins, and it’s not the satisfying kind. It’s slow, restrictive, and punishing. If you think this is a game where you’ll be spamming dungeons, farming gear, and smashing bosses, it isn’t. It’s a Platinum Simulator with a few extra materials sprinkled in.

The fact that this has a  mixed review is ridiculous. Before posting this review I want to give some details about me. #1 I really loved the anime (not the sequel but the original 7DS) #2 I played grand cross for years before finally walking away. #3 I played Genshin from day 1 for about 5 years before I got tired and quite that game. #4 I was excited about this game since it was first announced years ago. With that out of the way let's talk about the game.  For a game using the word "Origin" in it it is obvious that the people behind this game have no idea what that word means. Instead of creating an open world which followed the 4 seasons of the original anime they start you with Tristan who was NOT ONE OF THE 7DS. This game should have been titled appropriately "7DS The Knights of the Apocalypse" Although I have watched the first few episodes of that anime and this just seems to be an original story using the characters from the anime. Most who play an anime based game play it to play through the story they enjoyed watching. When games remove that their is absolutely NO reason to base it off that anime.  I expected a story starting at the beginning and gradually moving up. But instead I got a boring story that I just can't care about and ended up just skipping because the writers have NO  IDEA how to make a story as good as the original story. Game play: OMG this is awful, I run a pretty high powered rig that handles all AAA games smoothly and for some reason this game will drop down to 20 fps. Even when I turn off Vsync and cap the FPS to 60 I still have issues of it hitting 30 fps. Also the actions feel delayed. I will hit an ability and trigger it early when I want it to charge just because when I pressed the button NOTHING HAPPENED, the same goes with gliding, I have to spam the glide button. For a game that was in development for years this is disappointing. Overall they took an IP that could have been an AMAZING open world game and turned it into another Genshin Clone with a worse pity system. They took the pity system from Tower of Fantasy ANOTHER FAILED GENSHIN CLONE. Instead of learning from Wuthering Waves and using Genshin as inspiration and then making weak points of Genshin better they just copied it, made it greedy right from the beginning and slapped an anime IP on it to make it sell. It really is disappointing and all the positive reviews shows you just how many fan boys bought into this crap.  And if you think I am exaggerating how bad it is they actually had to release one of the most OP characters from the Anime so EARLY it makes no sense. They released Escanor before; Ban, Gowther, or Merlin. If you watch the anime you know that Escanor is the last knight revealed because he is insanely powerful. They released him early in hopes of using his hype to get the game more attention. I got him so I am not writing this out of bitterness for being shafted, but still it is obvious how badly this game is going when a trump card is used so early. If you do play this game be wary of spending money because I do not think this game will last a year or 2 with how bad it is now.  UPDATE 6/7 Been playing this game to give it a chance. I have had my PC shut off 3 times while playing this game. My pc has been recently updated and has never had this issue before not even with games like Nioh 3, RE9, or Black Myth Wukong. I had started to get hope for this game but then they released new hard content which REQUIRES a specific team. What makes this problematic is that getting resources to craft and level up the necessary weapons, armor, and accessories is a time limited and requires a lot of boring farming. You also need the newest premium character to even be allowed to join parties. What makes this worst is that they added a time limited event which gives you 10 premium wishes but you have to clear this raid 5 times. There is no lower difficulty offered. 7DS Grand Cross took A LOT longer to go P2W. This game did it within a few months. Their endgame is actually worse then Genshins Spiral Abyss. While my gacha luck has been good this just pushes me more to stop playing it.

The performance is completely broken. I’m running the best gaming CPU on the market and I’m forced to play on Medium just to get it to function. If you’re on a mid-range rig, don’t even bother.  The Castle is a disaster: The game runs poorly everywhere, but Liones Castle is a straight-up crash fest. It’s impossible to get through side quests without the UI bug and there is 1 side quest that is bugged by the Liones Castle.  Broken UI: There’s a tracking bug that forces every single mission onto your screen at once. It’s cluttered and shows they didn't even test basic systems before launching.  Damage Control: This "Dev Talk" on Steam is just pure panic. Netmarble is bleeding players and they’re only apologising now to try and stop everyone from moving on to games that actually work.  Unfinished mess: They basically admitted this version is "early." We are just unpaid beta testers for a rushed mobile port.  I just wanted to play the story and be done with it, but the game is too poorly made to even let me do that.  If people are having a blast then i'm happy for you but how? Playing on low fps and crashing alot is hard to play.  EDIT:  I’m updating this because the game finally had a patch that fixed the Liones Castle area. It’s no longer crashing every five minutes and the performance is actually stable now. I still think it’s a mess that it launched this broken, but at least it's playable. If you were holding off because of the crashes, it’s safe to go back in now.

3.5/10  + Incredible cel shaded 3D anime graphics. + Lush and vibrant environments. + Great optimization; stable 144 fps on max graphics settings on RTX 5070 with zero framerate drops and no screen tearing. + Decent action combat with good elemental-based mechanics. + Plenty of ways for character progression. + Each character can swap between 3 different weapon types further adding to party building freedom. + Decent character progression and gear system. + Enjoyable and rewarding open world exploration with plenty of activities to do and loot to discover. + Exciting sense of discovery; the map is not cluttered with filler POIs so it feels very rewarding to stumble upon treasure, environmental puzzles and challenging encounters by accident or thorough exploration. + Each character has a unique Adventure Skill that can interact with the environment & puzzles differently or help with traversal. + Enjoyable environmental puzzles. + No fall damage. + Satisfying traversal mechanics. + Sidequests are well marked on the map including the quest starter and objectives. + Auto Move available to easily navigate to the next main quest objective. + Pets with Auto Loot available to help pick up enemy drops. + Incredible OST. + Can freely switch the controlled field character. + Dynamic day/night cycle.  - Censorship; Tioreh's outfit was heavily censored/toned down post-release and doesn't match the early trailers and beta version. - Bland, unappealing female character design and skins. - Characters and weapons share the same gacha pool; too many male characters pollute the gacha pool and drastically reduce the probability of pulling an attractive female character. - Horrendous gacha rates; 0.8% for an SSR character and 6% for an SR character or weapon, 93.2% chance of obtaining useless R weapons. - No invert-Y axis camera option in settings. - Many UI bugs and inputs are frequently unresponsive on controller. - Knockdown and stagger animation is too long and cannot be recovered from, leading to getting animation locked and suffering unavoidable consecutive follow-up hits. - Progression is extremely slow and timegated due to the low amount of daily stamina which is required to claim rewards from dungeons and bosses. - Extremely annoying to be forced to go into menus to switch teams if your main party doesn't have a character that has the matching element to trigger environmental puzzles. - Sidequests are mostly boring and unrewarding fetch quests with too much backtracking. - Monster taming is too resource intensive and the success rate is too low. - Lack of basic quality of life features & accessibility settings. - The story is quite difficult to follow unless you're already familiar with the anime. - Boring story and characters.
Genuinely, a very sorry gacha. Feels soulless and just uninspired.

AVOID THIS GAME AT ALL COST.  Ive been waiting awhile before finally giving my opinion, and after 73 hours logger its not good.. this has to be the worst gotcha game launch i have ever experienced.  From the very start of the game your gunna be met with a pain fully slow start gear is hard to come untill later on. Once the main story is done youll be forced into only doing meaningless mindless fetch questing untill you hit lvl 40.. its mind blowning unlike any other open world gotcha not a single thing you do earns exp towards world lvl.. only quest. From having 3 weapon masterys your force to lvl even if youll never use the other weapon types in your life. To Needing 3 different boss mats for 1 upgrade. Then comes the fragments shards. Every boss can drop each type. (I went a full 2 weeks without getting a single one og the ones i needed) forcing me to be stuck with no progress at all. Got all the mats i needed but without the shards your just screwed.  Engraving sets: with many sets in the game youll find yourself farming out different content trying to get the gear needed to craft a specific character Engraving. All of this could be forgiven it it was for the fact you can roll healing or barrier stats on a character specific Engraving that doesnt even have the those types of ability leaving you SOL on days of farming if not weeks.  But the nail in the coffin? The pure amount of bugs in the game that actually physically stop you from doing content. As it stand i have been progression locked for 2 weeks because of not getting the drop chance on the fragments i need as i am F2P and cant afford to pay2Win them or the game being so broken dungeon is broken and freezes at a very specific point.   Bottom line the game should have never launched in this state and as this is net marble we are talking about i dont see them ever recoverying the broken trust and the piss pour game design.  I REPWAT AVOID THIS GAME AT ALL COST.

To do this however you need to play on higher world levels, where enemies are tougher, which you can hardly beat with a less built team, keeping you in a painful loop of hoping you can kill something by out damaging the enemies before you go down and praying you get a good drop to give your team another tiny boost. So have really been waiting for this game. And it's with mixed feelings.  The game is just buggy as hell and poorly optimized. And yes its your typical money over pulling gacha game.  The combat itself is somewhat fun, not horrible, but nothing special.  What is bad, is the needlessly complicated combo system, which i suspect doesn't actually work.  Then there are the vital combat mechanics that are just straight up broken. First, dodging is a 50/50 chance to actually dodge. Second, the character switching during combat serves several purposes.  2 of them, again, doesn't work, 50% of the time. So when the enemy does a wind up attack highlighted in blue, and you switch, its supposed to stun the enemy, this doesn't always work. Second, if you have been stunned, switching characters is supposed to take you out of the stun. This also doesn't work 50% of the time.  Then there is the myriad of other small but extremely annoying bugs and glitches.  And then there is the combat score/level. What is supposed to tell you how strong your team is versus whatever boss or challenge you are gonna do. Now this number is effectively meaningless.  In some cases yes it means something, and in other cases, it does not. Let's say your score is 15k. You go against a boss that is 12k, and you beat it relatively easily.  Then another boss can be at 11k. And you get absolutely stomped.   I just cant recommend this game until they fix all these issues.

The combat system is incredibly unfair when it comes to dungeons. It will recommend a team combat class of about 8,000 yet when I go to do the dungeon at a combat class of over 17,000 I die incredibly quickly and have been unable to beat it. Maybe it is because I cannot get away from attacks but the movement is flawed. With a boss that moves that fast and attacks so relentlessly and the clunky movement it's impossible to survive. either please let the recommended level be accurate to what is required or make other adjustments. I will continue to play because I think that the game has a great future but right now the combat can be incredibly unfair. It also makes it that much worse when it requires clearing that boss to be able to level up stats. But yeah, for now I don't recommend it. Please don't discount it completely for now because I believe it will get better in the future.

I do like the game, there are a lot of things it gets right like the world map, the combat, the weapon and armor progression system, and the story are all really good. Once you've gotten past the story though, everything begins to feel like a full on grind. The amount of materials gathered throughout the world is minimal for how much you need them and they can't be infinitely farmed because you have to wait a day for the spots to open again. The drop rates for boss and dungeon rewards are abysmal for the amount of keys needed to unlock the reward boxes. The animal catching concept is horrible because the rates for catching them are horrendous even when you have the highest possible rank capture potion. You can't even buy the stuff with ingame currency (like materials for crafting and stuff) and is not extremely but noticeably P2W (pay to win) compared to other games in this genre. These issues will hopefully be patched or reworked over time, but not anytime soon as the game is still in the "honeymoon" phase.  Edit: DON'T PLAY UNLESS YOU BURN YOUR WALLET FOR P2W STUFF! THE RATES ARE ABSOLUTELY INSANE FOR EVERYTHING AND IT'S HORRIBLE TO DO ANYTHING IN THE GAME WITHOUT FEELING ABSOLUTELY DRAINED

I played this game mainly to find out if I should be excited for the Shangri-la Frontier game that this same studio is producing (japan only as of writing) and it has disappointed. I'm not a fan of Seven Deadly Sins (sun axe guy is cool) so I'm not the target demographic when it comes to the story. But that didn't factor into me not recommending the game.   What did was how much of this game just feels like someone's take on Genshin Impact. Incredibly similar menus, gameplay loops, gacha and gameplay mechanics. I had more fun here than with genshin, but it feels like the skin of a franchise was placed onto the bones of another game. Like it doesn't do enough *different* to make me think of it as anything but a studio making a slightly improved version of another game  To add onto this, alot of the information about each character is just not easily acessable in-game. Wanna know what one of the two banner characters does? Google it chump, you can only try out the newest one. And ONLY try out, you can't click a menu to remind yourself of their abilities. (And if it is accessable in game, I sure as hell couldn't find it)

I'm changing my review again, I thought they would keep up fixing the issues. They aren't. Everything is calculated Server side, which means when under strain everything grinds to a miserable stuttered mess. It is painful to fight any of these rediculous bosses that hop from one side of the ring to the other because the game simply freaks out, frame rates plummet, and the game favours caluclating the enemies actions rather than your inputs. (I've heard it's even worse on mobile devices)  It's extremely poorly devloped Genshin copy. There is nothing new or exciting here. If you're a 7DS fan, what theyve done is tried to make a new story, but realised noone cares about the new characters and have then tried to fit "the memories you once loved" around this time travel idea. Don't eat the Memberries.  The latest content is designed for current whales, and implements AGAIN the enemy hopping around, causing frame rate to plummet and 1-shot killing you. Marmas is absurd.   The grind is ♥♥♥♥. Theyve given 50 key/stamina, thats 2 dungeon bosses and 1 world boss. Roughly 15 minutes of playtime and thats you done for the day. Unless you want to spend it mining or fishing (such exciting gameplay).  Don't be fooled by the hours played in my profile, this game gets left running for bosses to respawn or because I've got soooo bored I've just walked away.   I tried to support it, but even as a 7DS fan, this has nowhere near the excitement that GrandCross did. The bugs are still throughout the game to an unplayable degree in later bosses. Bosses themselves are Damage Cap events, where even if you can dodge, heal and survive it will still deny you because of an arbitrary timer.   Co-op?! What is the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ point when you only get 50 keys?! "Hey friends you all up for playing 10 minutes of 7DS Orgins?I swear it will be worth it!"   I am going back to my initial view of this slop. They've copy pasted Genshins engine (nothing new), then they've taken GrandCross models and overlayed them (STILL NOTHING NEW), theyve half baked a story and stuck you in quickly dying P2W, relatively pleasant looking world.  Oh also, Meliodas is the only character that can parkor over terrain, everyone else (including the literal flying fairies) are slow and useless.  Water travel is even worse somehow, so slow and pointless.  Many people, myself included are wondering how this could take so many years to develop and how it could still feel so rushed and half baked. It is hollow.   I'l monitor its progression, but at this point I think it's fundamentally flawed and playing now just upsets me. Their latest event is Enchanting (extremely expensive) which involves farming bosses, which are ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ miserable to do because of the above issues.
Smooth combat is a must for these games but this one does not have that. Just play Genshin.

tl;dr It's not very good. I wanted it to be but it isn't. It's not bad, however, either. I have no idea what they plan to do in terms of refinement, but it needs a lot. Environment 8/10, real effort was made here which is why the rest of the game confuses me. Characters 7/10, relatively true to originals for reference characters, otherwise who the heck are these people basically Story 3/10, bad, even for a gacha so far. Gameplay 6/10, needs polish  It's a clunkier, Genshin Impact with less love put into it. They need to polish the controls a great deal before I'd consider playing this over more streamlined gacha games. Not really gonna continue cause of several things:   -Mainly the clunky controls but also seeing some VERY low effort assets, and I kind of don't really see the point other than generic gacha cycle, which just flat out isn't why I play these games.    -Nothing so far in the game attaches it to 7DS other than the paint job, and like one tiny reference here and there, which I did expect, but somehow it still let me down on that front.    -The story is entirely incoherent so far to the point that I... can't read anymore. Have seen exactly 3 mentions of anything 7ds related and they are all characters mentioned in test. Nobody seems to recognize or care that suddenly there's a lake where there wasn't. I've fought 3 bosses and still am being brought through various tutorials.   -The art is actually not terrible, but there are other bits which don't seem to fit in with the rest of how things are designed, it'll be really hard to miss it if you've played other gacha games...    -There's also a glaring lack of things to collect and do, I've explored hundreds of nooks and crannies where any RPG veteran would think "Yeah definitely loot there", and it's just barren. Why did you make all these cool hideable areas and use almost nothing?  I get the idea behind it but a little more effort would've gone a long way across the board. This could've been good but once again, the effort was seemingly not made anywhere other than trying to pry the player from their money.  Since gacha devs don't seem to get it here it is in plain text: The reason people play your games and spend more money on your game than other gacha games is not because they have nicer art, or more predatory monetary practices, the reason they succeed is because they make a game that people WANT to play, with a coherent story and interesting characters. Saying, "hey give us money" at every turn from the start, while offering very little of substance to use that spent money on, isn't gonna cut it and isn't gonna keep people spending.  Good luck to the devs... I see hope here but I'm out of hope to give for gacha games. May future players have a better experience than I did.

Thought I would update my review to correctly show my current opinion of the game. First, I want to start with positives, because unfortunately the list is smaller than the problems I have with it.   My favorite part of the game is multiplayer with how open it is with the content it allows you to do with friends. It is really unlike a lot of other games in the genre.   Second to Multiplayer is the pets/mounts. I've gone around and caught basically every mount in the game and it was one of the most enjoyable aspects of the many hours I have spent on this game.   The third and final notable thing that I enjoy about the game is getting my teams up to the level their at now, with being able to solo all of the hardest content in the game atm (Besides TSJ Sector 2). I will note that I actually do have a big problem with how grindy it is to optimize the teams to solo abyss stuff considering abyss content is supposed to be grinded by the average player. But I am someone who A, has a lot of time to grind, and B enjoy getting on for hours a day, but most players will definitely find it annoying how much they need to do to be able to do the abyss and Time Space Junction.  Now officially onto things I REALLY don't enjoy and also why I couldn't recommend this game for the AVERAGE NEW PLAYER.   General Gameplay issues  I'm gonna start where I left off. The amount of grinding and systems you NEED to engage in to be able to do the hardest content is ridiculous. From SSR weapons on all your attackers, the correct SR weapons maxed out by crafting a bunch of random SR weapons to amplify dmg on support and bursters, use enhance beads on all your weapons and push for Attack rolls, farm the dungeons relentlessly to get all the gear and accessories you need, to  Engrave and pray that all 3 of the RNG stats are good. It is horrible for someone who wants to hop in casual and enjoy the content there is. And what hurts on top of all of that is, almost everything on this list takes a material called Platinum. Which the average player can only get 9.2 - 13.8 platinum a day depending on how lucky or unlucky you get (Having Daisy Changes the daily amount). 1 Engrave that has 3 completely RNG stats that can make or break the engrave,  is 25 Platinum. Engraving is 1 of the MANY systems that take Platinum btw. There are other minor material issues I have with the game when it comes to bead stuff but I won't get into that right now. This is a small gripe I have but please fix the targeting on switch ins, it is so annoying to switch in to stun and my character misses. One of the most annoying issues I have had is some world bosses are prone to leaving their own areas and resetting.     Bugs/Connection Issues I could actually write a whole second review just on this category alone. I've had bosses jump out of bounds and not come back, my character being completely stuck until I switch characters, enemies just not taking dmg from abilities ruining rotations, switching in and being sent so far to attack a target miles away. I've witnessed someone beat gray demon, then go to fight red demon, and when they switched characters at red they were sent back all the way to gray demon as if they did a tag switch (That's thousands of meters away btw). I've had the butterfly material just be completely invisible and the only way I could gather it is by tracking. Sometimes when I try to combine ult it just doesn't work. The new update has made it so on countless occasions I've fallen trough the map, to the point that I'm confident I could get on right now at the time of writing this and get under the map. I've had enemies blatantly hit me with abilities I was out the way of. Switch ins just not actually countering the enemy. As much as I like how open the multiplayer is, the connection issues almost ruin it. I have had multiple times playing story that I just couldn't see the cutscene but the person I was playing with could, Luckily I was playing right next to them so I got to see the story anyway but if I wasn't I would have missed a decent chunk of the new story. This List can actually go on for so much longer but you should get the point.   Something I hear people say is, "All games have bugs on release." this is so much different than almost any other gaming experience I've had for a couple reasons. 1 the sheer amount of bugs is wild, but the 2nd point is the most important, a lot of these bugs and connection issues effect the core gameplay of the game which makes it feel exponentially worse.  Issues I have with development I'm gonna start this section by giving the preface that this game was said to be ready 3 years ago and was in development for over 6 years(Over The average amount of time for a game like this), with a beta test. This fact makes everything I just said in the prior paragraphs worse in my eyes, because how after leading on the community for 6 years can you come out with a game this messed up. They had to add a system to show players what their engrave did before making it after launch. This is one of the most important decisions for people to make to upgrade their characters and you have no info on what it is I'm actually making. I can't stress enough how insane it is for the engravings to have 3 completely separate RNG factors that only one can be changed if you are a giga sweat at the game, and it changes the most useless of the 3 things. They have now said in these most recent developer notes that they are making a change to that. I'm sorry but I'm not gonna be one of those people who praises developers for fixes OBVIOUSLY glaring issues they created.

Pros:  Graphics, design, traversal, combat mechanics.    Cons:  Difficulty is not consistent.  Especially the bosses at the end of each Act, you have to either spam ranged abilities or dodge perfectly, as a few hits will KO. Healing is limited to 1 minute timer potion, and healer class requires you stand in 1 small spot for 10 seconds which is impossible as the bosses move frequently, and you have to keep moving to not get hit, so healing is pretty useless.  Also when you get KO'd (which will happen a lot) you don't regain your health, you have 50% health and need to find a health totem.  Makes re-trying a boss in the open world that much more irritating.     Recommendation:  If you like very challenging games where near instant reaction timing is required, drawn out boss fights (slow bleed damage), steep learning curve, nice graphics, and good character design, you will probably enjoy this game.    Things that would improve the game:  I do wish they would have brought down the difficulty in the story mode to be able to at least get to the end and start the end-game.  But as it is, I've hit the plateau at the World Level 3 challenges (even if I'm 1000 gear score above) I can't complete the dungeons in the time frame (on Hard difficulty for mastery dungeon the boss still has 75% HP and I run out of time), and I can't upgrade my character mastery so there is no way to make my characters stronger.    Also the healing mech, using up an entire character slot to drop a healing pod which barely works is not effective. Forcing people to farm and use potions, or have perfect dodge/reaction is a bold choice for an open world game of this type.

I have seen many complaints about this game that boil down to, "Once I complete all of the exploration and the story, the only thing I can do is use keys and build characters." That's par for the course with any gacha game. This game is free, and it has to make money somewhere. That being said, there are many issues with this game that have nothing to do with it being a gacha game.     	This game is full of a lot of bugs. It should not have been released in the state it is in, but I understand why they did it. This time of year is usually downtime for their main competitor, Genshin Impact. A lot of the game's systems are ripped from Genshin Impact. Some are improvements, and some are not.     Multiplayer:  	There are major issues with the multiplayer systems. You can play the main story with a friend, if you are willing to take the chance of missing entire cutscenes because the game skips them for you. For some reason, this happened to me many times, so I  decided to continue the story on my own. Because the system is there, though, it can be fixed and made into a very fun experience with friends. There are also issues when playing multiplayer for long stretches of time. Getting stuck in the ground after gliding is a common issue and requires a restart of the entire game because the reset player function only puts you under the map. When trying to join someone else, it always fails the first time and disconnects you from the game's servers. It usually works on a second try. Unlike in Genshin Impact, you can bring your whole party of four characters to another person's world, which was a welcome change.     Team Synergy:  	In the game's current state, there aren't enough characters. Unlike in Genshin Impact, this game encourages you to play with one element, with the exceptions of light and dark. This means that if you want to play a character, like Jericho or Mannie, then you have to play them together since they are pretty much the only cold characters. The problem is that they are only obtainable on the standard banner, and they are five stars. There are also few "cold" characters in the show, so unless the game adds more original characters, like Mannie, this element is less than ideal.    Original Characters:  	This is a personal complaint, but I don't enjoy the original characters. This is partly because they don't fit in with the story and partly because of their designs. The characters native to the game look incongruent with the characters from the show and movies. Their eyes have a lot of extra detail that Tristan and Tioreg simply don't have. I have experienced Mannie's story, and it is horrible; the pacing is way too fast, and when there was an emotional moment, I felt nothing. I barely know what Mannie is like, and the game did not make me care about her. Everyone I know who plays this game plans to skip any original characters for these reasons as well.     Weapons:  	The weapon situation is atrocious. There are no weapon banners as there are in Genshin. There is also only one type of four-star weapon that can be summoned. The amount of weapon types makes it so that there is a very low chance you will summon a weapon you actually want to use. I have no four-star staff or wands at the moment, and I know many people who are in similar situations. You can craft weapons, but that is a very late-game activity that I have not unlocked, even with 35 hours.     Generosity:   	The game makes itself seem more generous than Genshin Impact, but it isn't because wishes cost almost twice as much. In this game, summons cost 300 currency, while in Genshin, they cost 160. The amount given per chest in both games can be 5 at its lowest, but that means this game basically gives 2.5 of its currency per chest sometimes. Right now, this game is giving out a lot of free stuff, but I'm unsure of how long that will last.     Material Gathering:  	It's way too grindy right now. I have spent hours going from gold ore vein to gold ore vein. Every time I want to find a new one, I have to go into the crafting table, select a gold ingot, click on gold ore, click gather, and move to that location. Every time I want to find a new gold ore vein. There is only one at each location. There needs to be an easier way to track resources. Having the character Hendrickson helps because he marks resource-gathering spots on the map, but he is not given for free, and he doesn't distinguish what resource he's marking.     The Map:  	It's been very fun to explore the different places that the developers set out for the players; that being said, I do not believe it is accurate to the anime. The Fairy King's Forest can be seen quite clearly from Liones Castle. I also don't think the continent is shaped like Britannia.     The Good:  	The characters are modeled very well. You can get ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ for people if you work really hard and delve into the crafting system, though that would probably take hundreds of hours and many real-life days to do.  The multiplayer system is buggy, but the idea of it is phenomenal. I have yet to see a better system for any gacha game that has multiplayer. If this system worked, I probably would not be leaving this review. The creature-capturing system is fun. It's similar enough to Pokémon, but different.

My review I originally wanted to write is too long to fit into here, so I'll have to keep it short unfortunately...  I explored all maps in the release version, finished the story, tried building a team, but in the end as soon as you try to improve past a certain point, there is combat challenges you can not beat. That combined with the frustration from bad combat mechanics and me not liking combat overall, the game starts limiting you and you're unable to get any of the lategame materials. My characters already reached their possible maximum and can only go further up in mastery growth, weapon and armor levels, if I clear combat that I am not strong enough to make it through. I dont know why I care - I started the game for exploration and got my exploration. It was... okay, I guess. The story I wanted to give an honest try (not knowing anything about The Seven Deadly Sins), and most of the characters are written overly stupid or unlikeable. There's one cool NPC in the game that later transforms and turns ugly, so theres that. I don't know if Co-Op makes combat bearable, I'm not the kind of person to reach out to others to form a party, and I don't want to ask any of my friends to give this game a try because after 100h spent playing through the story and doing all the exploration... I don't want to force anyone I like to experience the despairs that this game brought over me. You can do everything you want but you will always feel underleveled and figuring out what to do to improve leaves you with confusion and disbelief. I would not play this game as of v1.0 unless you desperately crave map exploration. You will always find your way back to trying to improve characters, which will fail, because you're being blocked one way or another. I just feel hollow. 100h and I have no idea what to do with my characters even though I followed guides and suggestions and have an understanding of what they want you to do regarding combat in this game. As of right now, the developers seem to actively listen to player-feedback. The game was highly unstable at launch but several server maintenances already led to improvements. I don't want to outright doom-talk the game. There's a chance the devs just need more time to push the game into a good direction, but it's likely going to bleed through the playerbase for a while from here. Wishing the devs the best, but:  If you want good story writing: stay away.  If you want good map exploration: shape your own opinion, its doable.  If you want good combat but are no expert: stay away, this will get highly frustrating.  If you want good combat and are an expert willing to spend money: have fun with your 5-star team thats able to clear all content effortlessly.

We had a very promising game here, but the developers failed to deliver. Based on the well-known “Seven Deadly Sins” story, the game falls short in several key areas: - The world is vast but lifeless. NPCs mostly just stand still with no real activity. Even in large cities or castles, the number of NPCs is small, and as mentioned, they barely move. The interiors of these places also feel incomplete. Overall, the world feels empty and dull. - The current roster of gacha characters is limited, and surprisingly, there aren’t many familiar faces. Personally, I don’t enjoy playing as side characters from the main story. There are also very few female characters. - The puzzles are tedious and lack creativity. Recent games—starting with Genshin Impact and followed by many others like Wuthering Waves or Sword of Justice—also include puzzles, but they’re not this frustrating. Here, you often have to switch tool functions or use characters with specific elements (fire, wind, etc.) just to solve them. What if I don’t even have those characters from gacha? - Characters can use multiple weapons, and their skills change accordingly, but switching weapons is unnecessarily complicated. You have to pause the game, open the menu, go into the character screen, and then switch. It’s too clunky. Why not allow instant switching with a single button? Combat would feel much smoother.  Overall: I love the “Seven Deadly Sins” franchise, but this game is quite disappointing. I tried to keep playing in hopes of getting the characters I wanted through gacha, but after dozens of pulls with no luck, I ended up uninstalling the game.

Unless you are just really attached to SDS world and story, this is most likely going to be a pass.  The main characters just aren't fun or interesting.  Male lead is milktoast boring.  Just the standard anime hero trope.  The female lead is just awful.  Obnoxious and even abusive.  I don't find her antics fun.    Story just isn't very interesting.  It very much feels like they came up with mechanics for the game first, then just shoehorned in things that might work in the story to explain it.    Controls and combat are pretty clunky and unresponsive.  Positioning especially felt loose at times.    But worst of all is it fell into the usual gacha failings.  You get a free SSR or 5 star character, however they refer to it, along with a matching weapon.  And unless you are lucky with your pulls, chances are this is the strongest character you will have.  Significantly so.  Which is especially bad considering how power works in this game.  Your team has a single collective health pool.  So having one strong character can only carry you so far.    I'm sure if you dump a ton of money into the game and get the top characters you will have fun.  But as a f2p, prepare to suffer.

I've got 45+ hours in the game as of the original writing of this review, and I feel like I've seen enough to not recommend it to others yet. There are too many technical issues and missing QoL features that don't make it as enjoyable to play as it could be.  For positives, this game does animations and expression INCREDIBLY well. It is very faithful to the personalities you see in the show, and worth fully watching most cutscenes for.  Issues: 1) The camera is awful, especially in combat. If you're locked on to something and - like many of the enemies/bosses in the game do - it jumps across the fighting area, the camera takes  f o r e v e r  to catch up to it. Additionally, the environment and other enemies can obscure your view - there is no object culling, which is a huge issue in a game based on timing like this one.  2) The controller responsiveness is terrible - the game is controller-friendly (i.e. they have glyphs for it), but there is a strange lag with some controls that are locked behind random combat animations. This makes dodging extremely difficult to perform reliably, as your dodge will just straight up NOT respond, which is unacceptable.  3) Co-op is completely broken. I have tried to join my wife's world to help her with some quests/bosses, and it took about 3 tries each time I attempted to join up with her. Whenever I was finally able to get in, I couldn't teleport anywhere in her world. I was not about to walk the entire map JUST to get to the boss she needed to fight.  4) You can get softlocked in many instances. Puzzle pieces are missing/despawn for no reason, bosses in dungeons don't drop rewards and you have to manually quit/retreat, etc.   4a) Not a technical issue, just a complaint. This game is grindy. So ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ grindy. You will spend an inordinate amount of time grinding for fairly simple mats that barely level your equipment and skills. If that's not an issue for you, you may enjoy the repetition of it.  5) Ultimate animations do not give you any iframes. Some of them are considerably longer than others, at least in the context of combat time span. You will 1000% die to a hit during your ult animation if you don't plan well ahead for some of them.  6) Mobs reset randomly. You don't even have to go far at all from their spawn point for them to reset on you, negating literally everything you did in combat immediately prior.  There are MANY other bugs and complaints that I have, but these are the big dealbreakers for me.  Update: NONE of these issues have been fixed yet, FYI.

I had really high hopes for this game, but Seven Deadly Sins: Origins ended up being a huge disappointment. The graphics look nice at first, but that’s about where the positives end. The gameplay feels repetitive and uninspired, with missions quickly turning into the same grind over and over again.    The open world sounds exciting on paper, but it feels empty and lifeless. There’s not much to do besides running errands that don’t feel rewarding. Combat also lacks depth — it looks flashy, but there’s very little strategy involved, making it boring after just a few hours.    Performance is another big issue. I experienced frequent lag, frame drops, and occasional crashes, which made it frustrating to even play. For a game that clearly aims to be ambitious, it feels poorly optimized.    On top of that, the progression system feels heavily padded, almost like it’s trying to push players toward spending money instead of actually enjoying the game. That really killed any motivation I had to keep playing.    Overall, it feels like a missed opportunity. Fans of the series deserved something much better than this.

Honest Review – 6/10  I really wanted to love Seven Deadly Sins: Origin. The game is genuinely gorgeous — the character designs, animations, and world are some of the best I've seen in a gacha ARPG. The story was enjoyable, and the combat feels smooth and satisfying... at least while the main story lasts.  Unfortunately, once you finish the story (around Act 12), the game completely changes for the worse.  It suddenly turns into an extremely grind-heavy, co-op-focused experience that punishes solo players. At World Level 3, bosses become ridiculously tanky, many have cheap instakill attacks (like the Turtle’s Tidal Wave), and progression slows to a crawl. Weapon upgrading and gear enhancement feel like a worse version of Genshin’s artifact grind — lots of time and RNG for very little reward.  I’m 33 years old and I play several other gachas (WuWa, ZZZ, Genshin, HSR, Nikke, Arknights: Endfield, etc.). None of them make me feel this underpowered and frustrated 60 hours in. The other games respect your time and let you progress meaningfully as a solo player. This one starts to feel like a second job that heavily pushes co-op and endless farming.  I love the art and characters, but the post-story design is just not for me. If you enjoy hardcore grinding and co-op raiding, you might like it. If you prefer relaxing, solo-friendly gacha experiences where you can log in and actually feel like you’re making progress — this game will likely disappoint you after the story ends.

[h1]🎭 Beautiful World, Gacha Soul - A Mixed Blessing[/h1]  ⚡ Stepping into the world of [b]The Seven Deadly Sins: Origin[/b] felt like coming home to Britannia - the visuals stunned me, Liones city looks magnificent, and exploring as Prince Tristan brought back all the anime feels. But then the gacha system hit me like a Meliodas punch to the face. 🎰  🎮 The combat is fast-paced on paper, yet sometimes feels sluggish in practice - dodging doesn't always work when you need it most, and some boss fights feel more like RNG checks than skill tests. That said, the story is genuinely engaging if you're a fan of the series, and there's PLENTY of content to sink your time into without spending a dime.  🌍 Exploration is where this game truly shines - the open world is big, visually impressive, and navigating through different regions never gets old. However, some areas feel a bit empty, and you'll notice the [b]standard anime RPG formula[/b] repeating after a while - fetch quests, daily grind, the usual suspects.  ⚖️ Here's the truth: if you love gacha games and the Seven Deadly Sins universe, you'll find a lot to enjoy here. If you hate gambling mechanics or want tight combat, this might frustrate you. The game is free-to-play, so there's no harm in trying it yourself.  [b]💎 Verdict:[/b] Worth Your Time for fans - Skip This One for gacha haters.  The world and story carry the experience, but the monetization and combat quirks hold it back from greatness. Play it, judge it, and spend wisely! 🎯

It's so cookie cutter from everything else. It's a new gacha game riding the tail of a popular IP. The devs have little to no backlog of notable IPs, so it's tough to determine how this will play out in the long run. Will this be around in 2yrs using a non-original Netmarble IP? There is (currently) no road map. Every company wants a successful live service gacha game, but they are in an over saturated market now at this point with three more new big IPs in the works that have already gotten some hype. This game is another clone of things we have already seen. It's basically fan service for the fanbase of the IP. Honestly a thumbs up would strictly be if you haven't played games like Genshin or WuWa before or are really into The Seven Deadly Sins, otherwise, you are not missing anything if you main those games.  You do NOT need to know the existing IP to understand this game, the only thing that MAY be confusing is the character names and relationships, which the game quickly hints at and you basically just draw the pieces together yourself. This is like a side fanfic style story.  I personally (so far) did not run into any bugs and the game runs smooth for me. The VAs are all in Japanese and i'm not familiar with the language to comment on subtitle accuracy.  The game has a standard attack with two alternate attacks and an ultimate. You can quick swap between party members for some extra combos. The game in the beginning feels easy enough so far. But it also teaches you the basics pretty quick and if you've played any other gacha game, you know how tedious it'll get. Especially if this game isn't going to be your only gacha game.   The game really didn't need to be a gacha game. Outside of the first pulls you get for free, I haven't and will not interact with this games store. There are far too many gacha games and I'm already tied to one. But so far, I haven't gotten much in game currency to even pull once on the SSR banner. I don't see and promise though with others reviews about how stingy it is though. And gacha games can get pretty predatory and lure players into spending without them really realizing on how much. I was honestly hoping for a solid single player game in the seven deadly sins world that wasn't begging for my wallet. So this is a bit of a let down for me.  The game is free, so it doesn't hurt to try it out for yourself. You will get a clear understanding of how the game will play out for the rest of it's life cycle within the first hour.

The Seven Deadly Sins: Origin – First Impressions Review After spending about an 2 hours with The Seven Deadly Sins: Origin, my experience was mixed.  Played on the Playstation 5 for this. But my review and thoughts should still matter cause it's the same game just a different platform.  Visually, the game is impressive. The world design is vibrant, detailed, and clearly inspired by the anime in a way that feels immersive. Character models look great, environments are well-crafted, and the overall presentation gives a strong first impression. From a visual and artistic standpoint, the game does a lot right.  However, the progression system quickly raised concerns. Core elements of gameplay—such as unlocking characters and acquiring weapons—are tied to a draw (gacha) system. This means that progression is heavily influenced by RNG rather than player effort or skill. While this model is common in free-to-play games, it can create a sense that advancement is gated unless players invest additional money.  For me, this significantly impacted the overall experience. The early introduction of these mechanics made it difficult to stay engaged, as it felt like meaningful progression was tied more to luck or spending rather than gameplay itself.  Because of this, I chose not to continue beyond the first 2 hours. While the game has strong visual appeal and a solid foundation, the monetization approach does not align with the type of experience I personally enjoy.  RNG Rates: The draw system relies on RNG to obtain SSR characters. Limited-time banners feature specific characters, with some reports noting a 0.4% chance for featured characters, requiring luck or significant investment.  That said, players who are familiar with and enjoy gacha-based systems may still find value here, especially if they are fans of the Seven Deadly Sins universe.  Final Thoughts: A visually impressive game with strong presentation, but progression systems tied to gacha mechanics may limit long-term enjoyment depending on player preference.

Feels like an early alpha that needs a lot of polishing. Some environment textures are very low res. Movement and even the mouse cursor have small delays. The environment looks in huge parts very similar (generic grassland and beaches everywhere). The character movement speed is quite slow even with mounts. The "glider" sometimes doesn't active or activates with a weird delay. In some areas you can't use your mount and have to slowly jog around there. Most characters have little detail and just very mono colored plain looking clothes, maybe it's the same in the anime and at least accurate. Most playable characters look like NPCs.  I still enjoy the open world exploration aspects but what prompted me to write this review was "Molbog the poisonous toad": During the fight it did those super long and annoying spin attacks that just stun lock you for a long time while not doing a lot of damage. You just have to sit there and wait for this stupid attack to end. Such "waiting phases" are one of the reasons why I quit Tower of Fantasy, most bosses there had some annoying "flying around" or "going underground" phases. Also when I had nearly finished the fight the boss just spun so far that it left the arena, unfocused me and regained all its health. Like are you serious? It started roughly in the center, did no one test anything in this game and provide any kind of feedback?  It's a big and partially quite beautiful world and a lot of work went into this but there is this nearly constant feeling of "slop" and jankiness as if no player feedback was taken into account or as if barely any playtesting was done.  There are also a lot of small waiting times. Why does it take two seconds to open a chest? Why do I have to wait sometimes over a minute for crafting to get done? In one quest I had weird waiting interactions that lasted 5 seconds while the character was just standing around. Why? At least most dialogs can be skipped.  Some fights are also unnecessarily long. Not necessarily hard, just long and annoying.  Also the grinding of materials like ore and wood is quite annoying. Just don't require that many materials. Farming a bit is fine but don't make us have to do that much and spend so much time on such repetitive content.  Also please keep our item collecting pet around. It constantly has to be respawned for no reason which always takes two seconds for no reason and can't be done "during combat" for no reason.

I just want to leave a quick, honest (non-glazing) review after 3 days of playing. The game has a lot of bugs and the systems are extremely frustrating.  Party system is broken — constant disconnects. We have to keep re-inviting over and over again. Random bug where I just die mid-air while going in to fight Kira boss. Movement bug — sometimes I can’t move at all unless I switch characters once. When you die in a boss fight, your HP doesn’t fully recover. It’s incredibly annoying having to run back to a heal pillar every time. Can’t track materials from the Codex (only works at the crafting table?). This is honestly terrible — what’s the point of having a Codex then? If anyone knows a workaround, please tell me. The map is huge but feels empty — barely any monsters. Pet auto-gather system is annoying — why do I have to keep manually toggling it on/off? Just let it stay active. Character sometimes gets stuck in an animation (floating while walking). Only fix is relogging. Capture flask targeting is awful — slightly off the edge and it doesn’t register. Huge waste. Gold capture flasks cost real money but only give ~80% chance, and actual success feels way lower. Feels like throwing money away. Farming keys are way too limited (50?). That’s nowhere near enough. 100 would feel much better. When keys run out, the fun is basically over because other farming isn’t enjoyable. After failing or dying to a boss, there should be a retry button with full HP. Why force us to exit and re-enter every time? No overall damage summary after boss fights? That’s such a basic feature.  There’s more, but that’s all I can think of for now.  Overall: the Quality of Life is really bad. Simple things are unnecessarily tedious and frustrating. I’ve never played a game this buggy before.  That said… I’ve already spent quite a bit of money on it, so I’ll probably keep playing and wait for updates. Hopefully it gets better.

Rating: 5/10 (Mixed/Mediocre) Status: "Save your storage space."  "A beautiful wallpaper covering a hollow, cash-grabbing trap."  💀 The Roast (Why it hits 5/10): The "Genshin" Clone Syndrome: Calling this "inspired" by Genshin Impact is an insult to inspiration. It’s a blatant copy-paste of the UI, the stamina bar, the climbing, and the world interaction. Netmarble didn't try to innovate; they just tried to see if Seven Deadly Sins fans were gullible enough to fall for the same formula twice.  The "Damage Sponge" Nightmare: Combat looks flashy for the first 10 minutes. After that, you realize every enemy is just a massive HP bar that doesn't react to your hits. You’ll be mashing the same three buttons for 5 minutes just to kill a random bird in the woods. It's not "challenging," it's tedious.  Netmarble’s Greedy Hands: If you’ve played their previous games, you know the drill. The "Gacha" rates are abysmal. They give you a few free pulls at the start to get you hooked, then hit you with a paywall so high Escanor couldn't jump over it. It's Pay-to-Win disguised as an adventure.  Empty World Syndrome: Britannia looks pretty, but it’s "dead." You run across vast fields only to find a generic chest with two pieces of trash loot. There’s no environmental storytelling—just a map filled with icons to keep you busy like a second job.  Final Verdict: The Seven Deadly Sins: Origin is like a high-budget movie with a terrible script. It looks amazing in screenshots, but playing it feels like a chore. If you aren't a die-hard fan of the anime, there is absolutely zero reason to play this over the games it's trying to copy.  Summary: * Graphics: 9/10 (Gorgeous)  Originality: 2/10 (Plagiarism)  Gameplay Loop: 4/10 (Repetitive)  Monetization: 1/10 (Predatory)  Overall: 5/10 — "Play for 2 hours, delete, and go back to a real RPG."

The bad  • Extremely predatory monetization. Weapon skins are impossible to get as F2P. • Draw tickets get more expensive over time. • Some mounts can only be obtained through spending. • Material grinding is absurd for anyone who doesn’t pay. • No regular banner pulls after finishing the story and missions. • No events of any kind to keep the game alive. • Lots of bugs across quests, multiplayer and UI. • Enemies have no elemental affinities and there are no real reactions like in other ARPGs. • Original characters use a totally different art style, making the game feel like an asset mashup. • Every character has an adventure skill but the game disables them inside dungeons. • King’s adventure skill has a terrible angle that makes it hard to use. • Large areas of the map feel empty and underdeveloped. • Pets and mounts are almost all pointless with no unique utility or features. • The company, netmarble, does not compensate for the insane amount of bugs and issues the game has. The number one problem this game has is this company which is moved by greed.  The good  • Movement feels smooth and enjoyable. • The world map looks genuinely beautiful. • Multiplayer is great when it actually works. • Creature collecting system is nice. • Exploration is satisfying. • Boss battles feel solid and well-designed.

At the moment, the situation with the game is as follows. A bunch of the main bugs that directly affect the combat system, or simply movement in a dungeon (which is actually also very important, because almost everything in this game is time-based), still haven’t been fixed. Speaking about bugs in general, they do fix some things, but in return, twice as many new bugs appear.  As for gameplay, there is a total lack of resources for progression. Even with the large number of keys given through events, the situation does not change, because while currency can at least be farmed to some extent, this is simply impossible with platinum, which is needed absolutely everywhere and in large amounts. You can only get 11 of it per day, and on top of that, the developers have already said that they will not add more of it to the existing map, only in new regions. In one such region, they added a whole one piece over the course of a month and a half.  In addition, there are literally no activities in the game besides grinding various bosses and mini-bosses for progression. Nothing at all. They only keep adding events where, in order to get rewards, you have to farm those same bosses several times more. Zero content.  The game did seemingly add a new permanent speedrun mode, where once a week access opens to some special boss, and this should theoretically not be a bad thing, because these bosses give you the opportunity to experiment with team compositions, weapons, armor builds, and so on, but in reality only fairly wealthy whales can really enjoy it.  Because only they, through pure power, will be able to clear those bosses regardless of bugs that literally do not even let you dodge properly. And if you are F2P, then even if you have been playing since launch and haven’t missed a single day, if you understand the mechanics, know how to read and think, meaning you actually play well, then good luck even clearing the level with at least the worst time.  Because everything comes down to character ownership, their unlocked potential, and overall gear. Meaning that even if you got lucky with character pulls, maybe even spent a little money, everything still comes down to progression, which you simply cannot manage.  Not only does all of this grinding take an insanely huge amount of time, it also leads nowhere, because no matter how many other resources you have, you will simply always be lacking one key resource, and most likely currency as well.  And all gear here has to be crafted over and over again. For example, I spent two weeks trying to craft one decent armor piece for Escanor, but still failed, because the stats always roll into garbage, engraving is always level 1, and on top of that the bonus substats are bad too.  And the same situation applies to a bunch of other things, whether it is enchantments, which also require platinum, or even simply the ability to get some artifact from a boss, because a boss drops around 6 different items and only one per run, so I think you understand how difficult it is to get something like a ring, which also has a random bonus stat. And by the way, you can only do such a boss twice per day.  Another example: today I spent all the keys I had saved up, did the boss 11 times in total, spent a huge amount of time, and got nothing except gear materials for crafting, meaning artifacts were not even in the conversation, not to mention the specific one I actually need.  So basically, there is no reason to expect that in the near future you will be able to strengthen your character in any way, unless of course you are an unemployed millionaire with a huge amount of free time.  Simply put, I do not recommend the game.  At the beginning it was interesting. For a gacha, it looked decent enough, especially since it is based on a familiar universe. Despite the bugs, I thought things would change, but quite a lot of time has already passed, several updates have come out, and the situation is now more than clear.  Yes, in the future they may simplify some things a little, maybe add some content, fix some bugs, but in reality there will only be more grinding, new bugs, and just a huge waste of time.  I quit Genshin many years ago and literally hate it, but at this point I would rather go play that instead. Even there, there is more content, practically no bugs, and you do not have to spend your whole life and money just to clear one floor of the Abyss.

Edit: Okay, nevermind. The game's bugs have gotten far worse, and the devs haven't even started to address them. They've acknowledged a few, but haven't bothered to fix any. Instead, they released patch 1.1 with 'harder' content (hey, nobody wanted that, the combat was fine other than the horribly designed boss fights. If you're going to make harder content, at least get the combat to a point where it's not absurdly clunky first). I'm pretty annoyed that I spent so much time (and money) in this game. Don't make the same mistake I did.  The game's only been out for a few days, but it's been an absolutely fantastic time so far. My only regret is not rerolling more, but that's just my awful luck and nothing to do with the game itself. Its story is a blast, and I can't wait for more of it. It's the first gacha story that I haven't had to force myself to pay attention to or care about since 1.0 of HSR. The game and its world really feel like you're playing in the 7DS world. The characters are all well-written and a joy to interact with. Oh, and the OST is phenomenal, as well.  The combat is much more difficult than any gacha I've come across, but not in a bad way. It's a bit clunky-feeling at times, but I think that's more of it being different from the low-quality stuff we're used to in open-world gacha combat than it actually being bad. It's refreshing to have to actually pay attention to boss and enemy mechanics for once rather than button-mash to victory.  Honestly, once we get a few QOL updates, it's gonna be one of the best gachas out there. With its emphasis on combat, writing, and story, it's certainly pulled my attention from any others I'd had in mind.

I’ve tried hard to tolerate the flaws in this game, but Junction Timespace Sector 2 is where I draw the line. Whoever thought fighting two bosses at once was a good idea fundamentally misunderstands game balance. It’s not just double the stats, it’s double the attack speed and double the AoE. It completely eliminates any window to attack because you are forced to spend 100% of your time dodging and trying to stay alive.  I’m done making excuses for it. Poor optimization, poor design, and poor content. I dropped over $300 on this just to end up feeling miserable. Do yourself a favor and go play literally any other gacha game.

Game play: If you enjoyed Genshin impact you will like this game, with one impactful difference. This game has a tag system, where you use a button to switch to a difference character doing bonus dmg. Its almost like fighter game but with unlimited switching. For most of the stuff, story included you can get away without constantly tagging in and out. If you plan to play at the top level expect to get min maxing your ability to tag and who with.  Story: A bit paint by the numbers so far, but still enjoyable. There is nothing breaking new ground here, if you do not enjoy the gameplay loop, this would be a hard pass for you, the story is not worth the struggle.  ** The way I treat gotcha games is if I enjoy it enough to spend at least 20 hours in, I decide what the enjoyment was worth and spend that in the store to show support. For what its worth, I have paid its the game **

This game has its pros and cons, Amazing music, seven deadly sins IP, amazing details to the map. I was relatively surprised by them keeping something like Elaine's resting place. But that's about it, I was anticipating a amazing underwater experience as the trailer has shown before release and hyped for amazing character animations.  However, as many has mentioned, the controls can sometimes be wonky (underwater especially), feels like your WASD is confused on what direction they're supposed to go (presumably because of the mobile port), Bosses are scaled terribly, Albion is just a boring hp sponge, along with the buggy climbing to the core where you might lose stamina while doing so, if the albion gets stunned outside the arena, you won't be able to access the core as well. I periodically felt I lost frames at some parts of the game where it was still playable but made visible to me.  A huge gripe for me is the combat and skills, this has to be the most boring 2 skill + ult combo, I managed to rolled Diane & Drake, However their skills are so subpar. Drake's skill just look like ordinary auto attacks, Diane's kinda boring too, And trust me when I say this since I was a part of SDSGC's community and SDSGC had AMAZING attack and ulti animations. Game was overall left with problems of power creep to the point the community I once knew disappeared completely. I would expect the same with origins but yikes, at least do something about the animations.  FYI, you can strangely bunny hop by going into mount, press shift once and just press jump when you land, your movement speed will be so unreasonably fast, not sure if it's intended or not.  Well this game has potential and the seven deadly sins IP, but please netmarble, do not repeat another SDSGC.

Cons: - Another gacha game with all the predatory tricks: high initial one-time rewards, FOMO, gambling, etc. - Day-one unnecessary stealthy censorship without explanation or compensation. - Even though the game asks you to be 16+, the game world seems more childish than the original anime. - Starting MCs: Tioreh and Tristan are annoying.  Pros: - Seven Deadly Sins :) - Anime-style graphics. - F2P. - Open world with rich exploration. - Genshin / WuWa-like combat with character switching and some personal touches. - Similarities with existing games make it easy to pick up and master. - Runs and looks well on a good PC.

Honestly, this game is pretty bad. After spending nearly 29 hours playing, I’ve experienced a lot of what it has to offer and it’s mostly disappointing.  The combat system is mediocre and doesn’t bring anything new to the table. It feels just like any other generic gacha game. Exploration can be somewhat enjoyable at times, but it’s held back by basic issues, like the lack of a proper item guidance system. There’s no continuous tracking for materials, which makes farming feel tedious and boring.  The biggest problems are the camera system and the boss fights, which are very frustrating—if you move even slightly out of a boss’s range, it immediately resets and fully heals.  Additionally, the excessive skill effects obscure visibility, making it hard to see enemies.  The enemy lock-on system is also not well implemented and poorly designed.  Pros:  - Uses the The Seven Deadly Sins IP - Player-friendly gacha system - Resource farming for character progression is decent (especially if you have a party). However, some dungeons take quite a long time to clear, which may not be ideal for solo players.  Final Thoughts: Overall, the game is quite disappointing. That said, if the developers continue to update and improve it in future patches, I hope it can become better over time.

SDS: Origins is overall a carbon copy of Genshin Impact, and I don't mean this like "oh they have a very similar vibe and it's a gacha game" but rather it is like I am playing Genshin but it has been repackaged as Seven Deadly Sins. Everything going from the map - to mobs - to the menu design/layout. It's surprisingly similar - Liones giving a strong nostalgic vibe of Mondstadt itself. All in all, this doesn't necessarily mean it's a bad game.   The graphics of the cut scenes and scenery are absolutely beautiful - and the characters have more equipment slots and you can combine armour pieces to make full sets instead, along with a range of weapons to collect and seeing familiar faces pop up which you can also add to your character roster. That being said - combat gameplay and other things such as fishing, swimming and climbing are laggy, can be laggy atleast. This is a big let down and does impact the game a lot more than you think - it becomes very annoying. I, however, give this benefit of the doubt as the game has just been released so hopefully it'll be sorted a bit later down the line.   One of the main reasons I continue to play is to eventually play multiplayer with my friends - as I haven't tried out this feature yet, I will update when I do. I don't exactly hold a positive or negative opinion on this game, it's worth trying out and you'll probably enjoy it if you like both SDS and Genshin. This will be posted as a negative only because I could live without this game ever existing to begin with. If I were to lose all my progress tomorrow, I wouldn't bat an eye.

For like the five Sds fans who actually still care for this franchise after season 3, the game is pretty similar to Genshin impact, but it has a few different things, for example mounts and flying mounts, each character can equip three different weapon types,  However lets get to the cons first, you know that guy on the who's face is plastered all over the marketing, Meliodas? yeah, he's gatcha, your main character is actually his son who's name I forget cause I checked out in season three, and knew that I wouldn't like the new series,   Next menus with controller are absolute GARBAGE! you can kick back and play with controller, but I reccomend staying close by your mouse for menus, also no real pause while you're going through menus, I made that mistake and was hit taking me out of the menu,  Now onto the positives, you can skip most cutsenes so even if you hate the main character you don't have to listen to him a whole lot, you get some gatcha characters early, you unlock Howzer and Guila early, (Howzer through the story, and Guila through a welcome gift, so if you like her might want to download and get her fast.) the weapon variation is actually pretty big, we have, swords, two swords, greatswords, rapiers, lances, spears, axes, three point staffs, gauntlets, and considering Diane is playable probably hammers too(edit No there are no hammers, she uses axes, fists and a three point staff.), Meliodas is also in the gatcha right now, but he's probabbly in the more premium gatcha since I've got like six ten pulls in one of the gatchas, and haven't tried for Meli once,   As a neutral most of the characters seem to have skins, Howzer and Guila have their old knight skins, which probably have to be brought, weapons have to be switched in the menu and their doesn't seem to be a quick switch, which is kinda lame, and the characters seem to take their designs from the new series, meaning Howzer has a goatee you can't get rid off, (I used howzer a lot as an example, but that's just cause it's easy to tell with him what changed cause I got him early,) and netmarble uses some updated models from the phone game.  All in all it has potential to be the best Seven Deadly Sins game, but it's gotta beat Grand Cross, cause the other game sucked ♥♥♥♥, personally I hope we get to have mobile game characters such as Roxy of madness, and Valenti of Obsession, but I won't hold my breath,   Negative review cause I think it needed more time in the oven, and I don't like that the trailers have Meli, which is purposely there to deceive people into thinking he's the main character,   Also haven't played much, but the "Origin" in the title implies that we're going to play through the origins of the sins,  and no you're not playing through the origins, cause again main character is Meliodas's son, so unless some time travel ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ happens, this takes place during the new series. The game is not a complete loss but it becomes a question of why play this over other games.  The gotcha space is saturated with games.  This game has tons of rough edges clearly the result of lack of effort because I have played several netmarble games and clearly they can do better.  Companies want to make 100's of millions or even billions off gotcha titles yet they invest nothing to make a decent gaming experience.  So yes as a F2P game you could have fun, but the poor quality means you probably wont spend money meaning the game will not improve or even EoS quicker then other games with a very similar game mechanics.   Some notable issues  Various bugs.   1) I experienced an audio bug that did not reset even on shutting the game down.  It only fixed after I performed a summon.  Very odd.   2) Also several UI issues where you see an error message appear on screen.  Usually due to item collection or map waypoints. 3) A few game crashes with error reports and even an anti cheat issue where the game crashes and took me a few tries to reopen because of anti cheat.  Clunky UI and combat.   1) The combat is the definition of jank.  Lots of collision detection issues on terrain and bosses making the dodge system a mini game in itself.  Also you can stagger mobs with normal attacks and push them through terrain that can cause them to reset.  2)THE MAP SYSTEM IS GARBAGE.  If you search for items they appear on your map until you close the map. Really!!!!! I should memorize the location.  Why have a mini map at all.  Even if you track the items; when you collect anything, and I mean anything, your track target vanishes.  For a game that makes crafting and material gathering a focal point this is beyond bad game design.  Also the only way to track crafting mats is to put down a crafting table and search it through the items.  You codex does not work for this.
