I WANT TO START BY SAYING TWO THINGS:   First — I genuinely love The Seven Deadly Sins. It’s one of my favorite anime since I was younger. I love the characters, the world, and there’s a lot of nostalgia tied to it. Because of that, my expectations for this game were high.  Second — this isn’t my first open-world anime RPG. I’ve played games like Genshin Impact, Wuthering Waves, and others in this genre. So comparisons are inevitable — and fair.  With that said, even with all the nostalgia, I’m not going to sugar-coat this ! ______________________________________________________________  ❌ Combat The combat is simply boring, repetitive, and clunky.  It feels like a weaker version of Genshin’s system:  - less responsive - less impactful - noticeably buggy  There’s no real depth or satisfaction. After a short time, it becomes a chore rather than something enjoyable. ______________________________________________________________  🌍 World & Exploration  The world itself actually looks decent — I’ll give it that.  + Visuals are nice enough + Traversal is okay at first  But then the issues show:  - Climbing feels slow and awkward - Stamina drains too fast - Gliding exists… but you can’t even speed it up  There are ideas here (like air travel as fast travel), but they’re underdeveloped and feel half-finished. ______________________________________________________________  💸 Monetization (as expected… unfortunately)  It’s Netmarble — and it shows:  - heavy grind - Minimal meaningful rewards - “Generous” at the start… but only for basic summons  Limited banners:  - 120 pulls for guarantee - very little currency provided for it  This isn’t surprising, but it’s still disappointing. ______________________________________________________________  ⚠️ Overall State of the Game  This feels like a mobile game pushed onto PC, not a true PC experience.  - Lack of polish - Lack of depth - Recycled systems  There’s nothing new here. No innovation. Just a mix of mechanics we’ve already seen — done worse !!! _____________________________________________________________  💬 Final Thoughts  I love The Seven Deadly Sins. That’s exactly why I’m being harsh.  This game had potential — a strong IP, a rich world, beloved characters — and it feels like that potential was wasted. Instead of creating something meaningful, it feels like Netmarble took the license and built a generic, soulless product around it. No innovation. No passion. Just another grindy anime RPG...  Simply put... I leave... sad... dissapointed... and from what I heard from friends and close ones... many did...

This game is a complete and total mess that's only playable if you're a fan of the 7DS IP. If you're not, you're going to hate it, plain and simple. The game in it's current state fails to compete with the now-competitive Gacha market in practically every aspect despite taking 5 years to develop. The big cons are: 1. Combat is genuinely janky as all hell. So much so that at times the game is honestly unplayable. You constantly get blocked by bosses when trying to dodge and the quickswap mechanic will only work about half the time.   2. Characters completely define your experience with the game. Even if you are a fan of 7DS, having unique traversal skills being tied to a specific character is a cool idea in theory, but the fact that certain characters can wall-run or fly while others cannot will either force you to spend money on a mount, or force you to spend money on the character banner so you aren't miserable. 3. 7 deadly frames. This game is so poorly optimized that it feels like I'm playing an MMORPG in a town square with hundreds of other players spamming their most graphic demanding spell all at the same time. You will clip through walls, fall through the floors and lose frames if you dare to sneeze too hard while looking at your monitor.   This game is a genuine 5/10 if you enjoy the IP and a 2/10 if you don't.

Honestly, I really want to love this game, but it makes it hard.  As you go through the story, you start hitting hard progression walls. It really comes down to who and what you pull or if you pull at all. If you don’t get lucky, you’re stuck being underpowered or grinding forever.  The gearing is the most frustrating part. You need a good weapon to get stronger, so you either pull one, buy one or farm one. You're kind of forced to waste pulling resources in hopes of getting a good weapon and if you don't get one, well put your farmer hat on... It quickly turns into this grueling loop of “I'm weak, I need to farm, I can't farm because I'm too weak."   Honestly, almost everything in this game feels too scarce. Nothing Is generous. Looting, gathering, crafting, It all feels too little. Not to mention this is my third account because I was shafted on pulls/progression more than once.  tldr - Game is very stingy, you go from the frying pan into the fire. If you don't pull or get unlucky pulls, you're stuck being weak and can't make progress.   If you enjoy this game or keep playing, goodluck and godspeed.
